chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Danger - Danger To Life From Electric Shock Indicates Life-Threatening Situations Due To Electric Current. Failure To Follow The Safety Instructions May Result In Serious Injury Or Death. The Work Being Performed May Only Be Carried Out By A Qualified Electrician Who Has Been Trained On The System.
text: The RTVision.t V4 Sensor is installed in the robot cell. If a break occurs in the safety circuit of the robot cell during regular production operation, this may give rise to unforeseen continuous operation of the sensor lighting. This also causes a significant and permanent increase in the heating of the sensor housing, especially at a higher ambient temperature. The temperature of the sensor may also increase in operation modes with a long inspection time and simultaneously short shutdown time. To protect the hardware, the sensor temperature is monitored internally by the software and switched off if exceeded. The unintended continuous operation of the sensor is ended after five minutes by the internal safety cutoff. This time frame can be changed in consultation with appropriately trained and authorized personnel

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The system is used in the commercial and industrial sector and integrated in an existing system. The operator is therefore bound by legal obligations to comply with standards and work safety regulations and to observe the safety, accident prevention and environmental regulations valid for the area of application of the system. Claims of any kind due to damage resulting from improper use are excluded and the operator alone is liable. Furthermore, the operator is responsible for intercepting signals issued by the system as envisioned and to integrate these signals in his system control and process them. The image processing system is parameterized in accordance with the quality assurance thresholds prescribed by the customer/operator.  Subsequent changes to these tolerances are not the responsibility of the service partner.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Responsibility Of The Operator

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Precautionary Measures During Use Danger Of Electric Shock.
text: High voltage - Danger of electric shock. Some of the components operate under high voltage. Disconnect the system from the power supply for cleaning and maintenance. In particular, always switch off the strobe controller before carrying out any work on the sensor. Always make sure that there is no voltage present before starting work.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Precautionary Measures During Use Danger Of Electric Shock.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Observe The Supply Voltage Use The Device Only With The Specified Voltage.
text: Live cables Do not damage, modify, over-bend, twist or pull the power cable. Do not place any objects on it and keep it away from heat (insulation could melt). Damaged cables must be replaced immediately by trained staff  .
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Observe The Supply Voltage Use The Device Only With The Specified Voltage.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Warning - Danger Of Electric Shock And Fire
text: Workplace - Danger of electric shock and fire Do not use the device at locations with high humidity, high levels of dust or oily smoke/vapors. Abnormal conditions - Danger of electric shock and fire If smoke or noise develops, disconnect the device from the power supply immediately and contact the authorized service partner. Foreign bodies/liquids - Danger of electric shock and fire Never insert foreign bodies into the plug, slits or other openings. Never place drinks or other liquids on or directly beside the device. If foreign bodies or liquids get into the device, switch off the power supply immediately and contact the authorized service partner. Dropping/toppling and damage - Danger of electric shock and fire If the device should topple over, be dropped or damaged, switch off the power supply immediately and contact the authorized service partner.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Warning - Danger Of Electric Shock And Fire

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: You Must Not Reach Into The Rtvision.T System - Danger Of Electric Shock And Fire
text: Additional components may only be installed by trained and authorized personnel and must be approved in advance by the manufacturer. Dismantling - Danger of electric shock and fire Never dismantle or modify the device. To extend the hardware, contact the supporting service partner.

section: Danger - Electric Shock During Maintenance Must Be Observed Before And During System Maintenance
text:

working on the sensor (replacing the protective screen, screwing on/unscrewing). This especially applies to the strobe controller. Please see the electrical wiring diagram for details of disconnecting from the mains supply. Before disconnecting the power supply, close the application and shut down the computer.
· Before opening the computer housing ensure that the mains plug is pulled out and all
cables to external devices have been removed.
· When changing computer components ensure that this does not release any static
discharges.
· Never open the computer power unit. It does not contain components that require

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
If a device is not used for a prolonged period, disconnect it from the mains. If you replace or renew live cables, remember that the maximum cable length is 28 m or 30m. At least once per year: Remove the plug from the socket and remove the dust around the pins of the plug.  
Caution - Observe the light source Do not look directly into the LED light source! Obey your sensitive eye reflex or natural aversion reaction and do not look into the sensor lighting. Continuous or high frequency lighting can give rise to stroboscopic effects, which similar to shadowing, flickering or flashing can trigger epileptic seizures in at-risk persons.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Caution

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Note - Material Damage Operation Of The System
text:
· You should not make changes to parameters unless you have received instruction in
the meaning of the individual parameters.
· If you have made changes to parameters, do not forget to save or accept them.
· If you want to switch off the system, do not simply turn off the computer. First close
the application properly and then shut the computer down.
· When using high image frequencies, the software takes up considerable system
resources. The system is designed accordingly. However, additional programs and/or tasks can interfere with the inspection and teaching-in process and should therefore be avoided at all costs. If you have to run other software in parallel with RTVision.t then contact the manufacturer beforehand.
· Back up the system regularly, as the content of the memory could be lost or changed
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Note - Material Damage Operation Of The System

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Note - Material Damage Operation Of The System
text:
· Back up the system regularly, as the content of the memory could be lost or changed
due to a malfunction or repair of the device. To restart the system, you need at least the folders **Appl** and Ini in order to ensure a workable system.
· Do not subject the system to any excessive electrical or magnetic fields that could be
caused by electro-welding, for example.
· When operating the RTVision.t system, comply with the requirements regarding the
ambient temperature. Do not subject the system to excessive heat or cold. Ensure that there is always sufficient fresh airflow for the computer and control cabinet.
· Do not operate the system in a contaminated or dirty environment, or in damp or
heavily particle-laden air conditions.
· The device is suitable and has been tested for a power supply of 230V at 50Hz.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The RTVision.t system is used to inspect and document glue beads with digital imaging methods for errors, interruptions or deviations. To do this, before starting operation, the parameters for detecting errors must be defined in the application software of the RTVision.t system in accordance with the customer's specifications.  
· The product is state of the art and is safe to operate. · If the product is not used properly or not used as intended, this could result in a
dangerous situation.
· Unauthorized modifications and/or changes to the product are not permitted for
safety reasons.
· The RTVision.t system may only be installed, operated and maintained by trained
and authorized specialist personnel.
· No permanent workplace is intended on the system with this product. The product
can generally be operated in automatic/normal operation without permanent supervision.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 10 Intended Use

text: Electronic assemblies with integrated circuits are very sensitive to excess voltages and electrostatic discharges.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Caution - Material Damage Electrostatically Sensitive Assemblies Can Be Destroyed By Discharges Below The Human Perception Threshold. These Discharges Occur, For Example, By Touching An Assembly Or Its Connections. The Damage May Also Only Occur After A Longer Period Of Operation. Basic Precautions To Protect Against Electrostatic Discharges:
text:
· Ensure good grounding when handling assemblies (e.g. during maintenance,
replacing components, cleaning and similar work)
· Avoid touching contacts or electric components · If measurements have to be carried out on assemblies, those carrying out the task
must ground themselves beforehand, e.g. by touching a grounded metal surface or by wearing suitable ESD-compatible shoes.
· Make sure to always observe the occupational safety regulations at your workplace.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: This manual describes the application software RTVision.t  for use with one physical sensor (with fixed working distance) and is valid for RTVision.t Version 4.8. Because of different versions or specific on-site requirements, there may be differences in the content (including pictures) between this description and the system that is installed locally. The values in the picture are not data that you should use in your system as guide values. The RTVision.t system is dependent on the signaling control unit (robot, glue process, higherlevel PLC), which requires use of special technologies from the manufacturer depending on the complexity. These are described separately as regards the settings in the application software and should be obtained from the manufacturer directly, if necessary
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Important Information About Deviations

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The RTVision.t system is used to inspect and document glue beads with digital imaging methods for errors, interruptions or deviations. The system can use image sequence recordings to capture the entire glue bead and then inspect and assess it in sections inline during the application of glue when attached to the robot arm in accordance with the parameterization. To do this, before starting operation, the parameters for detecting errors must be defined in the application software of the RTVision.t system in accordance with the operator's specifications. Also note that the RTVision.tsensor has been designed for a defined area in terms of working distance and calibrated accordingly ex works. Deviations from the boundary conditions over and above the prescribed framework (see data sheet) may lead to a decrease in performance as well as the accuracy of result data and process precision
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Intended Use

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . RTVision.t is not a measurement technology system, rather can be classified under visual inspection systems. Tolerances are compared and evaluated using image processing technology and targeted algorithms. The tolerances set there determine the subsequent result and are the responsibility of the operator. The RTVision.t system may be installed, operated and maintained only by trained and authorized personnel.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements
text:
The bonding or sealing of components as a replacement or complement for other joining techniques is commonplace in a broad range of industries. Both methods can be used to join a wide variety of materials, often with the help of robots. Despite cutting-edge application technologies for glues and sealants, it is often not possible to keep process parameters constant. Since bonds and seals require high quality, automatic process control is required. The inline glue and sealant application inspection system RTVision.t delivers a high quality inspection. The user sets all the parameters once and then the inline inspection system performs a fully automatic visual inspection of the glue or sealant application directly during the actual application process.  
The .t stands for "TRACKING"
Note Unless otherwise noted in this manual, the term "glue" can also be replaced with "sealant".
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements
text:
Note Unless otherwise noted in this manual, the term "glue" can also be replaced with "sealant".  
The system inspects the following characteristics:  
· glue bead width · glue bead continuity · glue bead position relative to the applicator nozzle · glue bead position relative to a component edge (reference, option of parameterization) ·  glue bead distance from a component edge (double reference, option of
parameterization)
Hence, the system contributes to ensuring the rigidity, solidity, leakproofness of the joint and ensures a defined crash behavior. Performing the inspection during the actual application process avoids additional inspection times and bulky inspection setups.  
Note The term "CONTROLLER" is used in this manual to mean the robot controller or the feed controller (adhesive application controller).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements
text:
The image processing system RTVision.t communicates with the robot controller and/or feed controller, but it is always the subordinate system. RTVision.t must be able to communicate with the robot controller for the purpose of exchanging various signals and data. The signals are used, for example, to transmit:  
- Inspection results
- Start / stop events or - General system messages and data
Communications is via fieldbus systems (e.g. ProfiNET, ProfiBUS, Ethernet IP, CANopen,…)
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements
text:
Communications is via fieldbus systems (e.g. ProfiNET, ProfiBUS, Ethernet IP, CANopen,…)
Note Note that for parameterization in RTVision.t, the system must already be definitively functional. Robot speed, glue control and travel paths must be installed as defined in the production conditions so that final parameterization of the products can take place in RTVision.t. If the robot speed is changed, for example, following parameterization, the product must be taught in again, re-parameterized if necessary and the error map adapted to the new start positions of the areas.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 2 Introduction 2 Introduction 2.1 Short Description - Technical Requirements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The potential applications of the inspection system are dependent on the shape, color and surface structure of the component, the type of glue application, properties of the glue as well as the guiding of the nozzle during the application of the glue. A detailed feasibility study therefore has to be performed based on original components and glues prior to configuration of the inspection system. The sensor, which is positioned vertically with respect to the glue bead, should ideally be parallel to the surface of the component while the glue is being applied. Slight tilting of the sensor is possible (max. +/- 8°), but not usually practicable owing to the dependency on the component surface, the component geometry and the condition of the glue. Depending on the sensor model, a predefined working distance must be maintained over the entire inspection section
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Technical Requirements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . Depending on the sensor model, a predefined working distance must be maintained over the entire inspection section. Owing to external influences (e.g. strong light changes, different metal sheets, degree of oil contamination or basic contaminants), deviations can arise in practice in the image processing system. The light conditions in the robot cell must therefore no longer change significantly following the initial configuration of the system, as they could otherwise falsify the image recording. The RTVision.t system is an inline inspection system, which performs the image recording directly after the application of the glue, which means that the robot should be moved on approx. 2- 3 cm after the glue application nozzle is switched off in order to also record the end of the inspection track
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Technical Requirements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . 2- 3 cm after the glue application nozzle is switched off in order to also record the end of the inspection track. This area can be parameterized separately in RTVision.t so as to allow the complete inspection track to be checked.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Technical Requirements

text:
· The number of main modules has been reduced to RTVision.t server RTVision.t client
RTVision.t viewer
· The functions of RTVision.t export have been integrated in the server. · The statistics area has been extended to include trend indicators using arrow symbols
(parameterizable) - the layout of the simple statistics has been changed.
· Image sequences can be clearly identified from the path. · Image and especially error sequences can be loaded globally at any time for
reparameterization. This means that more than one faulty inspection can be analyzed.
· The glue definition area has been simplified in relation to parameterization. · The number of sampling points per system can be changed dynamically, the default value
has been increased compared with the previous version - the result from curves has been significantly improved.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: An application can contain several products / components that are selectively called from the higher-level controller via specific IDs. This means that the actual glue inspection is carried out in automatic mode. The glue bead is checked in the current image sequence in this case and the visual results are compared with the specifications and their tolerances.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Function Overview / Function Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The results calculated inline are displayed as OK, NOK or WARN on the server interface screen during the check by the server. A new feature is the display of trends when several defects are detected one after the other at the same location. Depending on the selected default setting, the direction of the arrow changes depending on the set number of components. The RTVision.t viewer is used to visualize inspection results and show them in specially prepared graphics - older data series that already exist can likewise be imported from xml report files.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The application normally starts automatically when the Vision control computer is turned on. If a manual start is required, start the program by double-clicking the <%PRODUCT_NAME%>
(server) icon on the Windows desktop (you will need to be logged in as a Windows administrator for this). The application always starts in automatic mode and is ready to perform inspections once loading is complete.  
The program can only be exited after entering a password. This must be set up via user management or by the administrator.  
RTVision.t (server) and client communicate with each other. If both programs are open and the connection data (IP) is correct, select **Connect**
to connect the client and server. If both programs are connected, the corresponding button in the client is highlighted in green
.

.  
If the application is in automatic mode, the RTVision.t (server) checks the product(s) using a parameterized application. If the software is to be shut down properly, e.g. for maintenance or repairs, select Exit program on the RTVision.t (server). Approval by user management is followed by closure of the RTVision.t (server). The client can be opened via **Unlock configuration mode**.  
Also select **Exit program** there and after the password-protected process has been confirmed, the RTVision.t client is also closed. If required, you can now shut down the computer.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 3.6 Switching Between Automatic Mode And Configuration Mode
text:
To do this, change from RTVision.t (server), which performs an inspection in automatic mode, to the  RTVision.t (Client), in which you can specify the precise definition of the application in configuration mode.  
You can optimize the parameters in configuration mode without interrupting the inspection currently being performed by RTVision.t in automatic mode. To do this, you need to switch from automatic (server) to configuration mode (Client). With the user switches to configuration mode after entering a password.  
TIP
Check repeatedly which application is currently being processed; this is shown in the server and in the client in the status bar.  
· Login: The user name and password are requested each time configuration mode is
started (server) or reactivated.
· To switch from automatic to configuration mode, click **Configuration mode** in

section: 3.6 Switching Between Automatic Mode And Configuration Mode
text:
The application file loaded in configuration mode is not automatically saved or adopted when the program is locked. This is done only with renewed transmission to the server; to indicate the need to do this, the Transmit button is highlighted in orange. If the connection to the server is interrupted, the button changes its name and prompts saving with the orange-colored Save button.  
· To exit configuration mode, click **Exit program** or select **File | Exit program** in the
menu bar.
Any changes not saved will be discarded. An appropriate message is shown first.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
An application basically contains the following parameter sets:  
· Products · Inspections · Areas · Contours / polygons · References · Exposure control
The hierarchy of the parameter sets looks as follows: You will find further information on this in: Directory structure / files
30
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 3.8 Application Structure

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Application File The Application File Combines All The Data And Parameters Required For The Inspection.
text:
Products Products are the first parameter group beneath the application file. A product basically corresponds to a workpiece type. Each application must therefore contain at least one product. If a station processes different workpiece types, they can be created in the form of several products within one application. Example: Product_01 = front left door; Product_02 = front right door. Two products were used in the illustration on the previous page. Each product is assigned a product ID (1- 255) that can be used by the higher-level controller to select the product.  
The component images used for the error map in automatic mode are also stored in the products. The glue bead can then be created in a subsequent step. You will find further information on this in:
40
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Application File The Application File Combines All The Data And Parameters Required For The Inspection.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Inspections One or more inspections are created within a product. An inspection is normally created for each glue bead applied to the component. Each product therefore contains at least one inspection. In the example above, the first product contains only one inspection while the second product contains two inspections. More than one inspection will be required if the glue bead applied to a component is not continuous. There is no need to create additional inspections when using different tolerances for inspecting specific areas within a glue bead. Inspections themselves can be divided into defined, freely selectable areas. References can also be created within an inspection. Any number of inspections can be created, however they are always subordinate to a product. They are therefore managed within a product
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . They are therefore managed within a product. Inspections are automatically saved when the associated product or the application is saved. You will find further information on this in:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
61
General information about inspections Areas Areas are created within an inspection. An area contains, among other things, the error tolerances and the image processing parameters required to locate the glue bead in the image. At least one area must be created per inspection, although there is no upper limit. If different tolerances are to be used within a glue bead inspection, this can be achieved with any number of areas. You will find further information on this in:
80
General information about areas Single references References can be created within an inspection, however they are not absolutely necessary.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the nozzle at specific points. This is done by setting references at these points. Any number of references can be created within an inspection. Well-contrasted cut edges on the component can be used as a feature for references. The basic condition is that the feature that serves as a reference cannot be altered. Error and warning limits are also created for references. A reference containing errors will lead directly to an overall result of NOK. Double references and help reference Double references can be created within an inspection, however they are not absolutely necessary. They consist of a primary reference and an additional reference and are used to determine the distance from the glue bead to another edge at specific points. This is done by setting double references at these points
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . This is done by setting double references at these points. Any number of double references can be created within an inspection. Any edges (e.g. component edges) can be used for inspection purposes. Error and warning limits are also created for double references. A double reference containing errors will lead directly to an overall result of NOK.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Furthermore, a so-called help reference can be set for the additional reference. It is required if the distance to the center of the glue in relation to the component edge set as a reference is to be checked at defined points of the glue bead. You will find further information on this in:
115
General information about references
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Overview

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Synchronization references can be created at the start of an inspection before an initial inspection - e.g. in an area - is performed. They increase the repetition accuracy of the inspections but are not absolutely necessary for an inspection. They are based on support points before and after the position. These support points are also taught-in images. Any reference at the start of an inspection before an initial inspection and defined in the direction of travel can be used as a synchronization reference. It is used to synchronize the position of the inspection system and the robot, which permits a more precise evaluation of the glue bead. A synchronization reference cannot be activated with **Quick teach**.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Synchronization References / Special Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The quality of the evaluation may be significantly impaired if a synchronization reference is not set up correctly. It is therefore necessary to ensure that the general conditions are adhered to when setting up synchronization references.  
You will find further information on this in:
115
General information about references
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Synchronization References / Special Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Exposure control is necessary if workpieces made from different materials (e.g. Usibor, galvanized sheet metal) with different reflective properties are to be inspected. This makes it possible to control the exposure time of the cameras and therefore the brightness within an inspection. You will find further information on this in:
76
General information about exposures
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Exposure Control

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The contours are used when the camera can no longer follow the robot guidance using the sampling points, e.g. when curves with narrow radii are to be inspected. Polygons are then set as contours at these positions, which can be recognized and assigned as contours by the system during the inspection. You will find further information on this in:
132
General information about contours
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Contours

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Camera arrangement The diagram below shows the arrangement of the camera images around the glue applicator nozzle. The red (dashed) line shows the switching threshold from one camera to the next. Images The sensor contains three cameras arranged at 120° to each other to provide the 360° degree view of the glue applicator nozzle required for the inline inspection of glue or sealant application. The images recorded by these cameras are simultaneously (PC) transferred to the vision controller (PC) over a Gigabit Ethernet interface at a frequency of 200 Hz (equivalent to 1 image every 5 milliseconds). At least one of the cameras is always behind the applicator nozzle to record images with glue.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 3.9 Image Structure / Image Sequences

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Function Overview / Image Structure / Image Sequences
text:
It can be seen in the diagram above that the camera images overlap. This is necessary to ensure reliable recording of the glue bead at all times.  
Image sequences The high image frequency results in a large number of images that are managed as image sequences in the image processing software. An image sequence can be considered a kind of film consisting of a large number of individual images. The path / time information for each individual image in a sequence is saved in an additional XML file for each sequence.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Function Overview / Image Structure / Image Sequences

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The methodology for glue bead detection can be configured in many ways to achieve optimum results.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 3.10 Inspecting The Glue Bead - Methodology

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
For RTVision.t a glue bead essentially consists of a pair of edges. The transition from light to dark (or vice versa) between two image regions is referred to as the edge. When searching for a glue bead, therefore, the system is actually searching for two edges with inverse light / dark transitions.  
103
During the search, all the edges that are detected are evaluated via rating functions with certain probabilities. The glue bead is formed by the two edges with the highest probability rating.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Detection:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Under certain circumstances it may happen that the sealant is applied in such a way that the inspection system cannot detect the edges. As an alternative to edge detection, an option exists to perform a gray level-based brightness analysis in designated areas. This makes it possible to inspect glue beads even without detecting edges. This checking method is useful for checking the continuity of the applied glue bead in places where there are no discernible bead edges (e.g. narrow housing flange or step seams).  
It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the component surface (i.e. the brightness analysis alone is not suitable for "black on black").  
In such an area, the glue bead is only defined using the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to check the width and position of a glue bead. For that reason, the inspection is limited to a continuity check.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Brightness Analysis:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The actual glue inspection is carried out within a configurable opening angle (from the glue nozzle) and within a limited selectable search radius.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Glue Inspection:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 4 Configuration Mode Description 4 Configuration Mode Description 4.1 General Information About Configuration Mode (Client)
text:
In configuration mode of the RTVision.t client, all products / components are parameterized for quality inspection on the basis of tolerances and reference characteristics. An application file is created, which can also contain several products and is transferred to the server after successful parameterization. The product overview in configuration mode (Client) gives you a quick overview of the contents of a product. It also lets you do the following:  
· Create / manage products · Create / manage inspections, including areas, references and exposures
· Display / modify the product image
194
· Load image sequences (see Image sequences
)
· Create inspection-related bead paths as well as the associated links to the product

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The image processing system can be used to inspect different components (e.g. "Left door", "Right door", "Tailgate", etc.), even if they are all processed at one station. The component types can change in accordance with production requirements. Each component includes a series of variables to be inspected (parameters).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.2 Client Screen View

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
An application can be created across systems during the course of commissioning by selecting **File | Local application | New** on the client. The application uses the default settings for System, Product and Inspection. The desired products (even more than preset) can now be created within this application. The default creation of products is important to initiate the storage of image recordings from the server side.  
When creating a new application, the system template comes from the server. The server remembers which products (by means of the ID) or inspections were selected by the robot BEFORE parameterization. Communication with the robot controller must work for this purpose, which is why it should be applied to the components for some time before parameterization. The server collects this data and then uses it for further parameterization.  
194
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Creating A New Application File

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
194
Tip - Recording image sequences The application file, products and inspections are created on the client, while the storage of the image sequence(s) required for parameterization is initiated in the server module. It is recommended to record images during all inspections. It is only if parameterization is not possible that recordings should be made here selectively based on inspections. See Server - Recording image sequences
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Creating A New Application File

section: Screen Layout Of Client Screen Layout - Main Toolbar At Top In Horizontal Orientation See General Information About Configuration Mode
text: Screen layout - Parameterization toolbar in horizontal orientation The buttons for the hierarchy within the parameterization are arranged here from left to right. The top level contains the products, which are assigned an inspection for each glue bead, which in turn is characterized by general settings, exposures, areas, references and contours (polygons). A vertical tab with the same name is displayed on the far left at the same time as the activated buttons.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Screen Layout Of Client Screen Layout - Main Toolbar At Top In Horizontal Orientation See General Information About Configuration Mode

section: Screen Layout Of Client Screen Layout - Main Toolbar At Top In Horizontal Orientation See General Information About Configuration Mode
text:
Lists the products that have been created within the application - the product level. The arrows to the left of this allow you to quickly browse through the listed products, including the display of the associated data. Lists the inspections that have been created for the relevant product - the inspection level. The arrows to the left of this allow you to quickly browse through the listed inspections, including the display of the associated data. Use of image sequences is mandatory at this level at the latest.  
Every inspection generally uses the globally applicable settings from the calibration. These settings can be changed via the **General** button or tab specifically for the selected inspection.  
73
75
See General tab and Inspection length This makes it possible to control and selectively change the exposure time of the cameras and therefore the brightness within an inspection.

section: Screen Layout Of Client Screen Layout - Main Toolbar At Top In Horizontal Orientation See General Information About Configuration Mode
text:
76
79
See General and Settings for exposures Areas are used to divide an inspection into any number of sections. This may be necessary, for example, due to different glue bead widths or changed light conditions. The error tolerance of an area should not be set too large so that deviations are detected in good time.  
80
96
See General and Settings for areas References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the glue bead at a defined position. Like areas, references must be created and taught in.

section: Configuration Mode Description / Client Screen View
text:
This function, which can only be set up as an option, allows the specific adjustment of the sensor's color channels before the product is inspected. Usage is intended only for products that cannot work with the global setting. This could be the case if different colored glues or poor intensities exist. Otherwise, however, the global setting is always preferable.  
Note - Ensure targeted use - Material damage possible This function should not be used lightly for any product, since it slows down the process and puts additional load on the strobe controller. The production startup takes longer and also the interval to the start of inspection is increased, so that the overall system performance could deteriorate if the parameterization is poor.

text:
To create a new product, click Add. A new entry with a default name and a consecutive ID will appear in the list of products. If the client and server are connected to one another, the system checks if data for the ID and the number of inspections exists for the new product in the **Presetting** window. If server and client are not connected to one another, the presettings from the server cannot be used. It is still possible to create applications or products without the presettings.  
The creation of new products or new applications on the basis of presettings is simplified considerably, however, with the use of server data.

The flexibility of the system is thus preserved. The ID numbers, product names and inspections can also continue to be finally reconciled against the data from the robot controller.

The loaded image sequence is displayed at the top of the inspection level on the right-hand side in the client window and the associated image navigator is arranged beneath this. The view of the image sequence with the navigator switches to the top center of the screen when one of the buttons for Exposures, Areas, References, Contours or General is selected in the parameterization toolbar.  
The **image navigator** is provided for navigating within the image sequences. The image navigator lets you do the following:

text:
Skip to the start of the image sequence Play the image sequence in reverse (play) Skip back one image Stop playback Skip forward one image Play the image sequence forwards (Play); repeated clicking accelerates the process. Skip to the end of the image sequence The image navigator also contains information about the current image number, the path and the time. The path and time refer to the "start inspection" command sent to the image processing system by the higher-level controller.  
If you click Play several times, you can increase the play speed incrementally, e.g. to run through very long image sequences faster - this also applies to the reverse play function. In single step mode, you can navigate along the image sequence by turning the mouse wheel. The image navigator is especially important for setting up the inspection tracks / bead paths.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 4.3.3 Configuring The Product Image And Bead Paths
text: The bead paths can be created in the product image with start and end point as well as important intermediate points. This error map is shown with the current glue bead for testing in automatic mode, thus allowing any possible errors to become evident during testing along with their position. You can insert the product image and bead path prior to parameterization and create the glue bead. The bead path can only be linked with the image sequence, however, **following** parameterization of the entire inspection to enable inline consideration in automatic mode in sync with the glue application.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.3.3 Configuring The Product Image And Bead Paths

section: Defining The Bead Path In The Product Image
text:
1. Select the product in the product list to which you want to assign the product image. By clicking **Load**, the product image (different file formats such as *.jpg, *.bmp or *.png are permitted) is loaded and displayed via Explorer.

2. Click **Inspections** to switch to the inspections list or open this list by double-clicking the product.  
3. To teach in the bead path, select the inspection containing the bead you want to teach in and then click
**Edit**.
Follow the editor tips as shown.
4. Finally, close the editor with

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence
text:
The bead path definition only tells the software the position of the bead in the product image. However, it does not yet know where along the bead (e.g. at 105 mm) to mark an error. That is why the bead path must be linked to the actual image data recorded. The start and end point of the inspection track must be linked here in particular. To do this, link the respective position in the product image with the position from the image sequence (values are taken from the parameterized areas).  
Tip - Linking the image sequence to the product image Do not set up the link until the areas have been parameterized and taught-in. The successfully calculated inspection length is also helpful for successful linking.  
If the images for the relevant bead are no longer available in the memory, you can load the image sequence belonging to the inspection manually.  
55
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence
text:
55
The position data, such as time in [ms] for the position, is then shown below the image navigator. Now switch back to the inspection and continue with the Image navigator to the start position in [ms] of the bead - the value corresponds to the start position from the first area created.  
Then move the red dot using the slider beneath the product image until it indicates the start position of the glue application in the product image. Finally, click the Add position button. The position link just created for this point appears in the link table.  
2. Proceed in the same way with the end point. Take the time data from the last area and switch back to the inspection list.
Use the navigator to move to the end of the image sequence and then move the slider below the product image to the end of the track.  
Add another position, this time the end position.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence
text:
Add another position, this time the end position.  
The inspection track is linked to the image sequence. If the glue bead is very long or if you want to increase the accuracy of the display (e.g. in curves as a result of different robot speeds), it is recommended to add further links at critical points in the glue bead between the start and end positions (next to drilled holes, lock areas, etc.). To do this, go to the corresponding position in the adhesive application using the image navigator, then move the red dot again using the slider until it indicates the selected position in the component image and click **Add position**.  
Tip - Synchronization If you want to test the links, move forward or back in the image sequence using the image navigator. If synchronization is successful, the red dot in the product image moves accordingly.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Linking The Bead Path With The Image Sequence
text:
Note The links created here have no bearing on the inspection accuracy. They are provided purely to show the errors in the error map in automatic mode. The errors are then shown with a red dot at the bead position.  
If you want to delete a link, select the corresponding entry in the *Links* table and then select the **Delete position** button.

text:
During an inspection, the glue bead is parameterized and the inspection tolerances are adjusted. Here you can:
Make general settings, for example define the permitted gap length for the individual inspection. Define and create exposures Create and edit areas Create and edit references Create contours and define details with polygons

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Please note that for parameterization in RTVision.t, the system must be already fully functional. Robot speed, glue control and travel paths must be installed as defined in the production conditions so that final parameterization of the products can take place in RTVision.t. If the robot speed is changed, for example, following parameterization, the product must be taught in again, re-parameterized if necessary and the error map adapted to the new start positions of the areas.  
Add To create a new inspection, click Add. A new entry with a default name in the defined local language appears in the list of inspections.  
65
Unlike products, inspections are not numbered via the robot controller. They are always executed in the order in which they appear in the list of inspections. More information here
.

Save Inspections are normally saved together with all the other image processing parameters in a common file with the extension .appl. However, an inspection can also be saved to a separate file with the extension .insp using Save. This inspection can then be loaded and used again later, for example also for a similar product (for more on this, see Directory structure / files
).  
Rename To rename an inspection, first select the required inspection in the list of inspections. Then click **Rename**. Change the name as required. Delete To delete an inspection, first select the required inspection(s) in the list of inspections. Then click **Delete**. Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes. Arrow up / down Use the two large arrow buttons beneath the list of created applications to change the order of the applications.

text:
Transmit application /
Save application Transfers and saves the current application file loaded in configuration mode (Client) to automatic mode (server). This button is active automatically when there is a connection between the client and server. If the inspection was configured locally in the client, the application cannot be transmitted, rather it is simply saved with **Save**. As soon as the connection has been established between the client and server, the created application can be transmitted. An inconsistency check verifies prior to transmission whether the application can run in the way it has been created and provides appropriate information on which configuration steps are missing. You will find further information on this in:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The manual check greatly simplifies the parameterization of the areas. It can be used to determine distances and angles (to the nozzle) directly in the individual images in the image sequence.  
To be able to use the auxiliary tool manually, an image sequence must be loaded and a calibration must be active.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Manual Check

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
To be able to use the auxiliary tool manually, an image sequence must be loaded and a calibration must be active.  
In the displayed image, you can perform manual checks in all three camera images. This gives you the option to determine point and distance coordinates. The point is checked manually with a single mouse click in the image, or the distance by clicking and recording the end point with the mouse button pressed. The result is shown beneath **Manual check** to the right next to the camera images either in table form or as a profile. This result is ideally suited for determining the width or brightness of the glue, for example, at every position. However, distances between the glue bead and the edge of the component etc. can also be defined quickly. Left-clicking in the image sequence opens a context menu for further actions (creating areas, references).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Manual Check

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The general settings can be accessed by double-clicking and opening an inspection in the inspection list.  
If the inspection is opened in this way, the *General* tab is always active.  
Similarly, you can use the button in the parameterization toolbar to select these settings. The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Opening The Tab

section: Controls Elements On The General Tab
text: Exposure time The brightness value for the inspection can also be changed here. This value then applies regardless of the value that was set globally in the sensor management. In order to be certain as to which inspection is using which exposure value, this function should always be activated even if the global and individual values correspond. Current calibration
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Controls Elements On The General Tab

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Changes can be made here if necessary if the calibration has to be modified. They should really only be pure optimizations, which are also reserved for the proficient expert.  
Gap length
Choose **Max. length** to set the maximum gap length permitted for each inspection.
Note that one level higher for a product you can also set the maximum gap length as a total of the maximum gaps of the individual inspections.  
Repair setting (optional) The repair function can be selectively activated / deactivated for the selected inspection using the **Repair inspection** checkbox.  
The repair function is an optional software enhancement and must be enabled separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-level controller must also be extended.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Inspection length taught-in The field is green if a length was taught in for the current inspection. In this case, the taught-in length for the inspection can be found in the **Inspection length** field. The set tolerance for the inspection length is shown in the **Fixed tolerance** field.  
Depending on where the software version is installed, the RTVision.t system can operate in time- or path-based mode. The lengths and also the times can be output accordingly in ms or in mm. The time- or path-based methodology must be stated from the outset. A subsequent change should be avoided as this would entail extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot controller (only KUKA) and in the image processing system.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

The latter renames the active areas for the entire inspection with the Adjust area names button with automatic numbering. The name is specified or defined in the **Default values** dialog - at the time it is created.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The inspection length is set in order to detect a bead path that is too long or too short.  
The output is in [ms] as standard, but can also be specified in [mm] depending on the specific customer interface definition. The time- or path-based methodology must be stated from the outset, and must therefore be agreed in advance with the manufacturer. Any subsequent change should be avoided as this would result in extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot controller and in the image processing system.  
The time required to teach in an image sequence is recorded for each inspection. The teachin process should therefore only be performed when all areas have been created and parameterized for the complete bead. A value is entered for the inspection length at the end of the teach-in process, which includes the full length of the glue bead. A system error
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.6.2 Inspection Length

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
(ERROR) will be output if the length of an inspection differs significantly from the length of the taught-in inspection. The maximum permitted deviation from the taught-in length can be specified by means of a parameter in a system file. This is taken into account as a **Fixed tolerance** in the length of the inspection.  
Values in the system file are system values that may only be changed in consultation with an authorized service engineer. To do this, the application must be closed and reopened, which is possible only during a break in production.  
Only the first inspection with an incorrect length is indicated, even if there are multiple incorrect inspections. This is because if a system error (ERROR) occurs, the rest of the bead is not checked.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.6.2 Inspection Length

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: An error message is output in automatic mode. This is displayed in the error information window. Only one error message is ever written. If the length of an inspection was identified as incorrect, the entire inspection is indicated in red in the component image. You will find further information on this in: Specific interface extension for path-based image processing as a special specification 237

text: Exposure control is necessary if workpieces made from different materials (e.g. Usibor, galvanized sheet metal) with different exposure durations are to be inspected. This makes it possible to control the exposure time of the cameras and therefore the brightness within an inspection. The exposure can also be changed or optimized within the inspection in a specific area of the inspection, since both can be interlinked by means of an identical start position.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
A distinction is made between two different types of areas in RTVision.t :
99
103
108
1. Active areas: in addition to the general Area definitions they contain all Search parameters and Error definitions that are needed to inspect a glue bead. Multiple areas can be created within an inspection so that different inspection parameters, such as also varying exposures, can be set. 2. Inactive areas: are used to exclude parts of the glue bead from the inspection or to exclude active areas completely. Inspection parameters do not need to be created for inactive areas since the glue application is not inspected in these areas. If the inactive area at the end of the bead is replaced by a checking method, a contour can be used at this point if necessary.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
For easier detection, glue bead detection is split into areas. Each inspection can work with several areas. These include gaps in the glue seam / glue bead as well as different widths of sections along the bead. Properties can thus be configured individually to suit the circumstances.  
To do this, select the respective area and click **Settings** to open the *Area name* configuration dialog.  
Note - Beware of making settings for multiple areas If several areas are marked, the button leads to global reparameterization. This ignores individually parameterized areas and overwrites all error definitions.  
91
The method described here assumes that the two edges of a glue bead can always be properly detected (edge pair detection). If edges cannot be detected uniquely, under certain circumstances, the method described in section Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis may be used.

The use of inactive areas allows the controlled inspection of a glue seam through to the end of the bead or the recording of gaps without checks being made. If you want to work with active areas at the end of the seam, define the parameters here with Contours

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
103
A search window is positioned in the camera image over the point where the glue bead should be. The system will only search for the glue bead within this window. The information about the position of the inspection track is acquired from the previous image or at the start of the bead from the user's specifications (see section Search parameters
).  
Each camera has a search angle of 120° available before the camera image changes to the next camera. The search range is always within the 120° of a camera. This approach is possible since the high image frequency means that there are very few differences between two successive images. Since RTVision.t inspects the entire area around the nozzle, the actual search area is shaped like a circle segment (see dark blue circle segments with the individual sampling lines).  
103
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Detection 4.4.8.2.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
103
For RTVision.t a glue bead essentially consists of a pair of edges. The transition from light to dark (or vice versa) between two image regions is referred to as the edge. Therefore, when searching for a glue bead / seam, the system is actually searching for two edges with inverse light / dark transitions. During the search, all the edges that are detected are evaluated via rating functions with certain probabilities. The glue bead is formed by the two edges with the highest probability rating. The width and position of the glue bead is then measured.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Detection 4.4.8.2.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Tip The default values for the glue definition can be used very effectively during standard checks for locating the glue bead. It is only if deviations arise because of shadowing or reflections that the default values for edge contrast or glue brightness should be defined more narrowly. The aim always is to keep the green circle on the glue bead. The error messages are not triggered by the search parameters, rather via the defined error definition values. The error definition is checked by the inspection run performed after the test and teach-in run. If the message "Glue not found" is issued, although glue exists, then the values for the search parameters have generally been defined too restrictively
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Detection 4.4.8.2.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . Make sure in particular with edge contrast and glue width not to align too strictly with the values determined in the test run, since any values calculated only reflect the mean values for both edges, with no separate consideration of the left or right edge.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Detection 4.4.8.2.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
This is performed five times (standard) per image using five sampling points. The lengths of any interruptions in the glue bead are also recorded.  
91
There is an option to activate a pure Brightness analysis area by area on the basis of edge searching and detection. The glue or sealant bead in such an area is only defined via the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to measure the width in this case. The brightness analysis can be carried out alternatively to or also in addition to edge detection.  
The wealth of information acquired about a glue bead enables RTVision.t to rate with a high degree of accuracy whether the glue bead is OK or not NOK. The main criteria for an OK rating are:  
· No interruptions (or a defined interruption length must not be exceeded) · Glue bead width within predefined tolerances · Glue bead position within predefined tolerances
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Detection 4.4.8.2.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Creating Areas By Means Of A Brightness Analysis 4.4.8.2.5
text:
Under certain circumstances it may happen that the glue is applied in such a way that the inspection system cannot detect the edges. As an alternative to the edge detection method described in the previous section, the option exists to perform a gray level-based brightness analysis in designated areas. This makes it possible to inspect glue beads even without detecting edges. This checking method is useful for checking the continuity of the applied bead even in places where there are no discernible bead edges (e.g. edge "merges" with component edge).  
It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the component surface under the glue bead (i.e. the brightness analysis is not suitable for "black on black").
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Creating Areas By Means Of A Brightness Analysis 4.4.8.2.5

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Creating Areas By Means Of A Brightness Analysis 4.4.8.2.5
text: In such an area, the glue bead is only defined using the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to check the width and position of glue bead here. For that reason, the inspection is limited to a continuity check.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Creating Areas By Means Of A Brightness Analysis 4.4.8.2.5

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Detection Of The Glue Bead By Means Of A Brightness Analysis
text:
A brightness analysis can then be activated area by area here so that the glue bead can at least be checked for continuity.  
1. To teach in the "edgeless" area, a glue bead must first be recorded, which is covered at
the problematic location with a material that contrasts with the glue in a clearly distinguishable way.
2. These images are parameterized as before using the standard method
.
83
3. An area where the brightness analysis is activated is then created at the problematic
location (specification of the positive and negative brightness deviation in gray levels %). 0% stands for black, 100% for white.
4. The application parameterized in this way is taught in.
5. The cover must then be removed before a second recording run. 6. An optimization run is performed with the second image sequence (see also Run
70
options
