In addition to the alternative *Brightness analysis* checking method, it can also be used as an additional method in certain cases. As an additional method, the brightness analysis can also be used for edge pair or single edge detection.  
This is useful if material or glue deviations occur occasionally in the glue bead which means that edge pair or single edge detection is not exact enough. With the additional brightness analysis, a defined measured width can be inspected via gray level comparison.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Detection Of The Glue Bead By Means Of A Brightness Analysis

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Edge Pair Detection + Brightness Analysis Combination
text: With this combination, edge pair detection is carried out first and, if glue is detected, a brightness analysis is also carried out. The settings for this are made for each area in the Area name dialog. Activate  **Additional method (Brightness analysis)** after selecting Edge pair detection from the **Checking method** field. The comparison is carried out on the glue width that is detected. If the comparison is unsuccessful, the glue is classified as "not detected". As with the brightness analysis, the brightness limits can be set here and also the maximum error rate on the Error definitions tab.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Pair Detection + Brightness Analysis Combination

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Edge Pair Detection + Brightness Analysis Combination
text: The brightness limits can be entered as relative percentages (in relation to gray level 0% =black and 100%=white) or as absolute values. Example of relative and absolute with an assumed average gray level of 60%: Relative limits with +/- 20% to 60% Absolute limits with 40% and 80% absolute to 60% For a **Detailed analysis**, a **Maximum error rate** is entered as a % that does not relate to the gray level % but to the number of results with a pixel-based review of the results. Example: per pixel the result is considered OK or NOK. If, for example, with 50 pixels at one point, 11 pixels are NOK, with an error rate of 20%, the overall result is NOK. In other words, the number of OK/NOK results per pixel is considered and this is used to calculate an error rate. This then produces the overall result OK or NOK. This error rate fine-tunes the evaluation even more.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Edge Pair Detection + Brightness Analysis Combination

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Single Edge Detection And Brightness Analysis Combination Configuration Mode Description / Inspections
text: In contrast to edge pair detection with brightness analysis, in this checking method the user defines the measured width for the brightness analysis. With single edge detection, a clear edge can be seen only on one side, the other side is, for example, the end of the component or the glue and material have the same color and do not create a contrast. By defining the inspection width, the inspection can be carried out successfully in the subsequent brightness analysis. Hence, the combination of single edge detection and brightness analysis is almost ideal in most cases because it is the most precise checking method.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Single Edge Detection And Brightness Analysis Combination Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Single Edge Detection And Brightness Analysis Combination Configuration Mode Description / Inspections
text: On the *Basic definitions* tab, the **Measured width** field is available when brightness analysis is used as an additional method.. The measured width defined corresponds to the length of the green sampling lines. The measurement direction is defined additionally by the combination of Glue appearance and **Edge transition**.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Single Edge Detection And Brightness Analysis Combination Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
In practice, the glue definition or edge pair detection can also be used for gap variance rating. Gap variance rating is simply inverse edge pair detection.  
This means that in the basic definition, the glue appearance defines what is or is not glue.  
If glue is detected, it is classified as "detected". Initially as a single image error, from a certain length it becomes a warning / error. The difference to edge pair detection is that here a fixed measuring start angle is defined and does not have to be taught in.  
Tip The start angle can also be determined easily from the image sequence by using the auxiliary tool.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Glue Gap 4.4.8.2.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Go to the starting point in the image sequence, calculate the bead width with the auxiliary tools and right-click **Create area** in the context menu to open the dialog shown including the value determined for the start angle. This can also be used to determine the start angle at the start of a glue gap. This angle is then valid for the whole area. If the start angle changes over time, several areas should be used to cover the entire section. With step seams, these gaps may not always be the same length. So as not to generate pseudo errors, inactive areas that are as large (long) as the glue process and signal inaccuracy should be used for the glue-gap and gap-glue transitions. To compensate for signal inaccuracy, a synchronization reference (special mode) should be used at the start of the inspection. You will find further information on this in:
83
68
129

text:
Basic settings If the **Profile** tab is activated, the basic settings represent the only option for changing the area parameters. The reason for this is that the **Profile** tab should only be regarded as a parameterization aid and does not actively intervene in parameterization.  
55
Start position Determines at what time in milliseconds or at which position in millimeters the area starts. The specification refers to the value specified in the Image navigator
. If the area is created interactively by marking with the cursor, the start position is defined and applied automatically. Area activated Checkbox activates / deactivates the area. The name is also indicated next to the area for better orientation.  
The settings cannot be changed if an area is inactive. An area can also be created as an active or inactive area interactively from the context menu by marking with the cursor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: An area that is active does not necessarily have to be already taught in. Information about the teach-in status is shown in column T., whereas the active / inactive indicator is shown by a green or gray lamp in front of the name of the area. If the lamp in column T. is red, the required teach-in run of an area marked as active is missing. The glue application is not inspected in inactive areas. Inactive areas are shown by a gray lamp in front of their name and in column T. They allow the image sequence to be recorded smoothly up to the end of the bead since they are defined, but not checked, 2-3 cm beyond the end of the bead.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

Arrow keys The arrow keys can also be used to scroll within the area list. Switching quickly between the areas of an inspection with the same screen layout is thus helpful for quickly checking parameters. The display of the last selected tab remains, but with customized content for each area. OK Click OK to save actions / changes made and close the window. Cancel Click **Cancel** to discard actions / changes made and close the window. You will find further information on this in:

Note that only the angle of the first image may be adopted for the start angle. A manual attempt elsewhere leads to this message:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Go to the starting point in the image sequence, calculate the bead width with the auxiliary tools and right-click **Create area** in the context menu to open the dialog shown above including the value determined for the start angle. A new area was created at the same time. Name assignment and further parameterization can follow. Five sampling points The standard number of sampling points is set to five and algorithmically adjusted to improve the evaluation reliability of the system. Sampling distance The distance between the individual sampling points can be defined. This can improve the evaluation of the bead in certain radii. The maximum possible distance between sampling points is 2.0 mm. The sampling distance is depicted by the distance between the green lines (5 lines) derived from blue circles of evaluation on the image sequence. The smaller the distance, the more precise the scan
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . The smaller the distance, the more precise the scan. Camera selection Specify whether the software should select the camera automatically or whether a specific camera should be used. Normally, this option can be left in automatic mode.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: When using a specific camera, the system will not switch to another camera if the glue crosses the switching threshold between the cameras.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Edge pair detection The glue bead is delimited here by an edge pair. The limit values are defined by the parameters on the *Search parameters* and *Error definitions* tabs.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Checking Methods

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
By activating the **Curve inspection** checkbox, the system uses a different algorithm in curves to optimize implementation of the width determination. Edge pair detection can be further improved by marking the Additional method (Brightness analysis) checkbox. Single edge detection If this checking method is selected, you can restrict the inspection to one edge of a glue bead in the relevant area. The selection of the edge to be inspected (left or right) is dependent on the setting under Edge transition on the **Search parameters** tab. Single edge detection can also be further improved by marking the Additional method (**Brightness analysis**) checkbox. The single edge detection is useful when the glue bead is close to another seam and only the removed side forms a clear contrast with the background.  
A width inspection is not possible with this setting.  
92
91
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Checking Methods

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
A width inspection is not possible with this setting.  
92
91
Brightness analysis This can be carried out as a stand-alone checking method or in combination with single edge detection or edge pair detection as an additional method. For details, refer to section Additional method (Brightness analysis)
.  
As an alternative to edge detection, it is also possible to carry out a pure gray level-based brightness analysis. For details, refer to section Brightness analysis
.  
95
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Checking Methods

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
.  
95
Gap inspection Gap inspection allows inspection of places on the components that must contain no glue so that they can be processed further. A typical application case is the inspection of step seams, although this can also be covered with inactive areas and the standard methods. In the case of active areas, but with the selective checking method **Gap inspection**, a NOK message is output if glue is detected in the sampling area. For details, refer to section Glue gap
70
Reflection filter The reflection filter can be used to minimize reflections on the glue bead and the sheet metal. The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be. If the reflection filter is preset to Off, no reflections are eliminated. The reflection filter can be optionally activated in the run options (see section Run options
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Checking Methods

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: ). The effect is most obvious with an activated filter in individual step mode of the currently active area. If you change the filter value, for example, between 2 and 50, in the window above, the filter effect is interactively visible in the displayed image. The filter size defines the number of pixels that are grouped depending on the filter type. The larger the value, the lower the reflection, but also the lower the edge detection.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Checking Methods

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The pixel values can be processed with three different filter types:  
| Automatic              | The system automatically selects the appropriate filter type for   |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| processing.            |                                                                    |
| Open (Opening Filter)  | Bright interfering points on dark surfaces are removed.            |
| Close (Closing Filter) | Dark interferences on a light background are overwritten by bright |
| pixels.                |                                                                    |
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
With a high filter value (max. 50), even actual edges in the image become blurred. In the current image sequence, the filter effect can be observed directly and can be selected appropriately.  
Glue appearance For clear differentiation in relation to the gray value analysis, you have to specify whether the glue in question is a dark glue on a bright background or a bright glue on a dark background. The immediate effect on the image sequence can be observed in the same dialog. This should always be used.  
If you change to **Glue brighter as background**, remember to also change the quality settings.  
Edge transition Here you can specify which edge transition should be used (from bright to dark or vice versa).  
This option should only be set additionally when **Single edge detection** is selected.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 >

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
103
RTVision.t uses rating functions to identify the pair of edges corresponding to the glue bead from the multitude found in the image. These rating functions are described as the "glue model".  
The settings for the rating functions for every inspection are made in the respective area in Area name window on the *Search parameters* tab.  
The glue model defines the possible properties of the glue in the respective area. This is necessary so that the software can select the correct edge pair belonging to the glue from the multitude found in the image. For example, it defines which widths could qualify as glue. If, for example, different application speeds result in the glue width within an area increasing from 3 mm to 6 mm, the glue model will define the entire range between 3 mm and 6 mm as a possible glue width.  
69
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Glue Model 4.4.8.3.3

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
69
The maximum and minimum values are shown in the Extreme values display
.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Glue Model 4.4.8.3.3

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The parameters or the rating functions for glue variance, glue width, edge contrast and glue brightness for glue searches are defined on the Search parameters tab in the Area name window. The software needs these parameters to distinguish the glue and/or the glue edges from a component edge, for example. Accordingly, the rating functions differ according to the checking methodology. Thus, with single edge detection, only the position and the contrast are to be evaluated.  
89
In order to reliably find the glue bead from the multitude of edge pairs visible in an image, the system first has to identify all the edge pairs in order to weight them using the abovementioned rating functions. The basic principle is described in section Inspecting the glue bead
.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Search Parameters 4.4.8.3.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
.  
The weighting takes place in the four rating functions, using four support points in each case. The position of these support points as well as the rating at the support points can be changed. The software calculates an overall quality rating from the four rating functions for each edge pair. This overall quality rating can be seen in the screen area on the left in the client during the teach-in run. These support points can be defined with two different methods:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Search Parameters 4.4.8.3.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Simple Definition With Symmetrical Data Structure
text: The default includes the simple method using tolerances that are to be defined with a value in plus/minus direction. This definition method is the tool for unique glue quality, clear edges and symmetrical data structure. In the case of symmetrical rating functions, in particular, this **simplified definition** can be used especially on the basis of numeric values as a definition tool. Variations with the same value are possible for each parameter with +/-. A value difference between + and - does not apply here. Equally, the quality rating also cannot be influenced.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Simple Definition With Symmetrical Data Structure

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note - System file settings If necessary, contact your service partner. Independent changes in system files lead to exclusion of liability. Once you have decided on the detailed definition, it is no longer possible to switch to the simple method as the symmetry of the parameter settings has generally been canceled. Working in parallel with both methods is not possible within one system.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Detailed Definition For Experts (Optional)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: With up to 32 parameters that can be set, this definition type is significantly more complex. It is the ideal tool for the complex glue bead and for experts in parameter evaluation. It offers greater scope for parameterization including quality variance. To edit the rating functions, default values are already provided by the system, which are displayed when creating the area. A small interactive default setting can be made if the area was created manually with the mouse (left mouse button) from the image sequence via the context menu **Create area**.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Detailed Definition For Experts (Optional)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The values for contrast, width and brightness are then already preset and can be adjusted simply to the specifications. In terms of position variance, the default value from the system applies, since this rating function cannot be determined interactively. It is also necessary to make manual corrections here. Another possibility to find default values when new areas are being created is to first assign previously created default data records for each inspection. Even then, parameters must be set depending on the test values and specifications.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Detailed Definition For Experts (Optional)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Glue variance rating - Find position Rates the position of the glue. For example, the system presets the values stated below, which must be adjusted manually depending on the test values / extreme values. Edge contrast rating - Find contrast Rates the edge contrast between the substrate and glue. This value cannot be determined using the auxiliary tool. Even though a contrast is specified, if it is used then the system is generally very restricted in its search values and tends to issue the error message "Glue not found". If faults like shadowing or reflection then arise, delimiting the edge contrast is a promising adjustment option that can be applied successfully with caution. 100% is the maximum contrast. See Edge detection Glue width rating - Find width The width of individual edge pairs is rated here. This makes it possible to exclude edge pairs that are too far apart
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . This makes it possible to exclude edge pairs that are too far apart. First, determine the actual width of the glue using the auxiliary tool (left mouse button) and estimate by how much the glue width can vary. These upper and lower limits should be given a quality rating of 1. All widths above or below this should be assigned a quality of 0, for example, but intermediate values can also be set. Example: The glue application should be 3 mm wide. The average width, however, is 2.5 mm to 3.5mm. This produces the following rating function:

| [mm]    |   2 |   2.5 |   3.5 |   4 |
|---------|-----|-------|-------|-----|
| Quality |   0 |     1 |     1 |   0 |  
68
Tip - Using auxiliary tools The actual spread can be estimated using the Extreme values display
.  
Further details can also be accessed on the **Profile** tab by left-clicking in the image sequence.  
Glue brightness rating - Find brightness Rates the brightness of the glue.  The value of 0% corresponds to black and 100% to white. The values in between correspond to the gray level between dark and bright. This option is visible only when edge pair detection is selected. The latter can be adjusted for the respective inspection in Area name | Basic definitions | Checking method.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Contrast and brightness gradients can be determined and displayed graphically for manual checks in the loaded image sequence. The profile display is purely an auxiliary tool and always opens with a blank graphic, even after exiting with OK. This graphic is displayed in the **Manual check** section and originates from the image sequence and from the path drawn with the mouse for determining the gray value or rather its contrast and brightness gradient. To help identify the point at which the system has already calculated contrast and brightness gradients, this information is displayed at the bottom of the glue profile section. They refer to the position where the sampling points are evident in the glue bead. For critical inspections, it may be helpful for further parameterization to look at the individual sampling points.  
97

97
Note that although the units of the x-axes are identical, the scaling does not match because lengths or offset are displayed as different parameters.  
In addition, in the standard table
(top right), the values determined when manually creating the profile are entered in the **Manual check** table.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Profile 4.4.8.3.6

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
A test run is recommended after you have created areas. For this test run, all the options under **Stop options** should be activated. This ensures that problems caused by e.g.  
incorrect parameters will be identified. You should also activate the reflection filter.  
Incorrect parameter settings can happen especially when areas are created manually. If this happens, the glue bead may not be found at all or the wrong width may be determined. If all the options under **Stop options** are activated, then the test run will be stopped if the glue is not found. You can then take corrective action. Incorrectly determined widths will not cause image playback to stop. This error must be identified by the operator. The corresponding error messages are displayed below the navigator for this purpose.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Identifying And Correcting Parameterization Errors 4.4.8.3.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
If the test run stops, you should proceed as described below. Each time you make a change, test whether it has a positive effect on the problem and whether it might result in any negative effects somewhere else in the glue bead. Here is a list of the most common parameterization errors:  
· The area starts in the wrong place
Check the *Start position* parameter in the *Area name* window under Basic settings.  
The start position can be shifted here directly or via the **Areas context menu** via a relative shift in the ms range (+/- entry possible).  
· Playback stops directly at the start of the area
If playback stops directly at the start of the area, check whether the **start angle** is correct. You will also find this information in the *Area name* window on the Basic definitions tab.  
· No glue found when switching from one area to the next
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Identifying And Correcting Parameterization Errors 4.4.8.3.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
· No glue found when switching from one area to the next
Check whether the *Start angle* is correct in the *Area name* window on the Basic definitions tab. Test your changes directly in the adjacent image sequence. Use the navigator here to follow the progress over the glue area and observe the behavior of the sampling points in the camera view.  
· No glue is found when switching from one camera to the next, the search area is not
above the glue.
Check the search area and modify it if necessary on the Search parameters tab. You can check your changes at any time using the *Test* button.  
· No glue is found within an area with one or more sampling points.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Identifying And Correcting Parameterization Errors 4.4.8.3.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
· No glue is found within an area with one or more sampling points.
Check whether there are reflections on the glue bead. If this is the case, increase the reflection filter (e.g. between 2 and 40) in the Area name window on the Basic definitions tab. If the glue bead merges with any seams or edges, the reflection filter setting is too high.  
You can check your changes at any time using the **Test** button.  
Calculate the width at the current position using the mouse (auxiliary tool). Check whether this width is covered in the **Glue width rating** function.  
You will find the rating function in the *Area name* window on the *Search parameters* tab.  
If not, adjust the rating function accordingly.  
Calculate the average gray level of the glue at the current position using the mouse (auxiliary tool). Check whether this gray level is covered in the Glue brightness rating function.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Identifying And Correcting Parameterization Errors 4.4.8.3.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
You will find the rating function in the *Area name* window on the *Search parameters* tab.  
If not, adjust the rating function accordingly.  
If the image looks very fuzzy and the contrast is low, check the Edge contrast rating function. The edge contrast cannot be determined directly using the auxiliary tool. The extreme values for contrast are, however, logged in the extreme values display. Check whether the values listed in the extreme values display are covered by the Edge contrast rating function. Another useful tool is the additional **Profile** tab, where you can use the mouse to determine brightness and contrast gradients in the image sequence.  
You will find the rating function in the *Area name* window on the *Search parameters* tab.  
If not, adjust the rating function accordingly.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
To teach in areas, the areas must be created successfully. Areas are taught in as follows:  
1. Switch to configuration mode and select the appropriate product / inspection.
2. Load the required image sequence. 3. Reset the extreme values display by clicking Reset. 4. With the exception of Single check, mark all the boxes on the **Run options** tab.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.8.4 Teaching In Areas

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
5. First start a test run by selecting **Test run** from the list in Run options | Operation mode. This is the best way of finding incorrect parameter settings, since image playback will stop immediately at these points. 6. If the test run completes without any problems, check once again whether the values displayed in Extreme values are within the values set in the area settings in Glue definitions. 7. If this is the case, select **Teach-in run** from the list under Run options |  Operation mode to start the teach-in run. Let the image sequence run until the end so that all the areas are taught in.  
Note The teach-in run must always be performed, otherwise the inline inspection system will not be able to inspect any components.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.8.4 Teaching In Areas

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Tip In the area list within the inspection, check whether all areas of an inspection have been taught in correctly; the green signal confirms the status after teaching in. If the signal in the T. column in the list of areas fails to come on, this indicates that the corresponding area was not taught in; for example, inactive areas are displayed in this way. The signal turns red if the teach-in run is faulty or has failed.  
8. Now start an **inspection run** to verify the teach-in run. To do this, select Inspection run from the list under **Run options | Operation mode**.  
As soon as the teach-in run has been selected, the system asks whether the teach-in run should be started. When confirmed,  all areas are taught-in immediately.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.8.4 Teaching In Areas

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the glue bead at a defined position. Like areas, references must be created and taught in.  
126
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.9 References 4.4.9.1 General

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: In addition to the existing functionality of references, double references can be used to determine the distance between two edges, e.g. the distance between a glue bead and a component edge to the left or right of the glue bead. They can also be used to determine the distance from the end of a glue bead to a component edge in the direction of travel of the glue application system (by means of a double reference). Double references work with a primary reference and the additionally defined reference as well as the required distance checks. A triple reference allows the distance between the component edge and the center of the glue to be checked with a help reference supplementing the additional reference (edge distance of a component to the center line of the glue bead)
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.9 References 4.4.9.1 General

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . Synchronization references, or special mode as it is referred to, can be used to avoid a delayed start trigger by the synchronization references acting as special references for an inspection.  This is done by using the position of a synchronization reference to compare the calculated distance and the distance actually covered.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.9 References 4.4.9.1 General

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Opening the tab To access the list of references, go to an inspection, double-click to open it and click the vertical *References* tab on the right-hand side of the screen.  
Similarly, you can use the **References**
button in the parameterization toolbar to select these settings. The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client. Move the mouse pointer to the camera view image that shows a reference and the glue bead. Select the component edge and left-click to choose **Create reference** in the context menu.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.9 References 4.4.9.1 General

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Select **Create reference** in the context menu with the left mouse button. Copy Use this button if you want to duplicate a reference. The copied reference is initially given a default name (this can be changed). Load To load a reference, click **Load**. A selection window then opens. Click the file to be loaded followed by **Open**. Save To save a reference, click **Save**. A selection window then opens. Assign a name and click Save. Delete To delete a reference, first select the relevant reference(s) in the list of references. Then click Delete. Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes. Rename To rename a reference, first select the required reference in the list of references. Then click Rename. Change the name as required. Move Opens the **Move start positions** dialog to set the start position of the active or inactive reference precisely

text:
Note - Marking multiple areas will result in global reparameterization Note that the **Settings** button will only lead to the Reference dialog if a single reference is marked. If several references are marked, this leads to global reparameterization.  
The general parameters (teach-in settings) can be found in the  *Reference name* dialog in the lower part of the screen and on the right-hand side of the dialog. They are always included in the dialog, regardless of which tab was selected to parameterize the reference (framed in red).  
55
Start position Determines at what time in milliseconds or at which position in millimeters referencing should take place. The specification refers to the value specified in the Image navigator
.  
Reference activated Activates / deactivates the reference. If a reference is inactive, the definitions for the reference and errors cannot be changed.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
A reference that is active does not necessarily also need to be taught in. This must be done by means of an additional teach-in run.  
130
You will find further information on this in section Teaching in references
.  
Teaching in references quickly The fully configured reference can already be taught-in after parameterization when the reference is created. Quick teach-in is not absolutely necessary, since testing is always carried out with the teach-in run, which takes place after the test run. If this teach-in run has already taken place and something still needs to be corrected afterwards for a reference, the quick teach-in phase is preferable for this single reference. The complete teach-in run of the inspection across all elements is then no longer necessary. Special mode is an exception, as it can only be taught in via a teach-in run.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The primary reference defines the image processing parameters for a "standard" single reference. The parameters are required to define the position of the reference in the image. A reference basically consists of an elongated search area whose position and length are defined by the start and end points of the search area. The system searches for edge transitions along the search area in a similar way to when the glue bead is being inspected. To this end, it is necessary to define whether the edge transition goes from "dark to bright" or from "bright to dark" and, in instances where multiple edges are found, whether the first or last edge found should be used.  
68
Start point Defines the start point of the search area based on the angle and the distance to the nozzle. These values can be determined via the Auxiliary tools
.  
68
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Primary Reference 4.4.9.2.3

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
.  
68
End point Defines the end point of the search area based on an angle and the distance to the nozzle. These values can be determined via the Auxiliary tools
.  
124
Check edge variance The box is checked if the distance between two edges is to be measured by means of a primary and additional reference (see section Additional reference
). At the same time, an offset (edge deviation) of the primary reference is inspected like a "normal" single reference. This box must be unchecked if this is not to happen.  
124
The box can only be checked / unchecked ifAdditional reference was first activated with the checkbox **Distance check - Check edge variance**. If the Check edge **variance** checkbox is grayed out, nothing can be selected.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Primary Reference 4.4.9.2.3

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Edge transition Here you can specify whether the edge transition (generally component edge) is from "dark to bright" or from "bright to dark". Edge position Here you can specify whether the first or last edge found should be used for evaluation purposes. The result of the settings is immediately visible in the adjacent image of the image sequence.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Primary Reference 4.4.9.2.3

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Minimum edge contrast Specifies the minimum edge contrast that must be achieved in order to detect an edge transition. Reflection filter The reflection filter can be used to minimize reflections on the glue bead and the sheet metal. The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be. If the reflection filter is preset to Off, no reflections are eliminated. If you change the filter value in the above window, the filter effect becomes interactively visible in the currently displayed image with the reference. The filter size defines the number of pixels that are grouped depending on the filter type. The larger the value, the lower the reflection, but also the lower the edge detection. The pixel values can be processed with three different filter types.  
| Automatic              | The system automatically selects the appropriate filter type for   |

Glue appearance For clear differentiation in relation to the gray value analysis, you have to specify whether the glue in question is a dark glue on a bright background or a bright glue on a dark background. The immediate effect on the image sequence can be observed in the same dialog. Since the primary reference mostly refers only to the edge transition of the component, this setting does not play such an important role at this point and an effect cannot be observed in the graphic view.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Additional Reference With And Without Help Reference 4.4.9.2.4
text:
The additional reference likewise defines the image processing parameters for a reference. These parameters are required to determine the distance between two edges in an image in combination with a primary reference.  
68
Start point Defines the start point of the search area based on the angle and the distance to the nozzle. These values can be determined via the Auxiliary tools
.  
68
End point Defines the end point of the search area based on an angle and the distance to the nozzle. These values can be determined via the Auxiliary tools
.  
Activate distance check Activates / deactivates the additional reference for measuring the distance between two edges.  
128
If the Activate distance check function is deactivated, it is not possible inError definitions for error or warning limits to be specified for distance variance.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Additional Reference With And Without Help Reference 4.4.9.2.4

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Additional Reference With And Without Help Reference 4.4.9.2.4
text: Edge transition Here you can specify whether the edge transition is from "dark to bright" or from "bright to dark". Edge position Here you can specify whether the first or last edge found should be used for evaluation purposes. Minimum edge contrast Specifies the minimum edge contrast that must be achieved in order to detect an edge transition. Help reference - Check distance to the center of the glue Activate this checkbox if a help reference is to be used in addition to the existing additional reference. The objective is to determine the glue center (from the additional reference) and its distance from the component edge (from the primary reference). This type of reference is also called a triple reference because three different reference types are used for one image in the image sequence.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Additional Reference With And Without Help Reference 4.4.9.2.4

section: A. Creating Two Single References As A Double Reference In The Same Image

1. First, carry out the steps described under Creating a reference

2. Then mark the primary reference just created in the list of references. 3. Click the **Start** and **Test** buttons to see the primary reference in the image (this is not absolutely necessary, but makes it easier to create the additional reference).
4. Mark the area where a search is to be performed for the additional reference (search area) using the mouse (auxiliary tools). The search direction for the additional reference matches the direction you drag the search area. The length of the marked area represents the search window where the system will search for the reference edge. The start and end point of the marking is indicated by a thin red cross and thin green cross respectively.  
5. Left-click and select **Additional reference** from the context menu.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The error definitions specify which tolerances will result in a warning or a NOK during an inspection in automatic mode. Warning limits, (maximum) edge deviation If an edge is found within the search area and its position differs from the taught-in position by more than the value entered in this field, this will result in a warning message. Error limits (maximum) edge deviation If an edge is found within the search area and its position differs from the taught-in position by more than the value entered in this field, this will result in a NOK message. Warning limits, (maximum) distance deviation If two edges (primary and additional reference) are found within the search area(s) and their distance differs from the taught-in distance by more than the value entered in this field, this will result in a warning message
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Error Definitions 4.4.9.2.6

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . Error limits (maximum) distance deviation If two edges (primary and additional reference) are found within the search area(s) and their distance differs from the taught-in distance by more than the value entered in this field, this will result in a NOK message. The following applies: Warning limits can only be parameterized with a lower value than error limits.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Error Definitions 4.4.9.2.6

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
A reference can be used as a synchronization reference. This type of reference compensates for any positional inaccuracies caused by the delay in the trigger signal (to start the inspection) coming from the robot. Ideally, carry out synchronization when the component edge is easily recognizable and at a 90 ° angle to it even before the start of the glue bead.  
If edge offset in a lateral direction of motion is more important, the primary or double reference is used. In this case, however, the time delays are not compensated for, since the relevant reference edge runs parallel to the glue bead here. The time window of a synchronization reference must not overlap an area or a reference.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Mode / Synchronization 4.4.9.2.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: A reference is created in the primary reference, where the component edge is still before the start of the first area. The component edge must be at a 90° angle to the glue bead so that the reference search area is identical to the running direction of the glue bead (see picture) Now select the **Special mode** tab to switch to the synchronization.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Mode / Synchronization 4.4.9.2.7

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Synchronize inspection If this box is checked, the reference can be used to synchronize the inspection. This enables repeated inaccuracies to be compensated for.  
In addition, a new teach-in phase is triggered; quick teach-in is not possible here.  
Number of support points (Synchronization window) This value indicates where the synchronization reference should be found when the selected number of images before and after the taught-in center is considered. The number of images in two directions creates a time/path window that must not overlap with an active area or reference. Four support points mean, for example, images of 20 ms before and after the centrally placed image are included and considered.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The number of support points is limited by the robot speed and by the size of the camera field. In the case of the synchronization reference, the entire sequence must be taught in at the end. Since a quick teach-in does not work here (corresponding button grayed out), the entire inspection must be taught in via a teach-in run.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
References are taught in as follows:  
1.  Switch to configuration mode and select the appropriate product / inspection. 2.  Load the required image sequence.
70
3.  Just select **Single measurement** on the Run options
tab in the *Stop options* area.
4.  First start a test run by selecting **Test run** from the list under Run Options, Operation mode.
This is the best way of finding incorrect parameter settings, since image playback will stop immediately at these points.  
5.  Select Teach-in run from the list under **Run options, Operation mode** in order to start the teach-in run. Let the image sequence run until the end so that all the references are taught in.
The teach-in run must always be performed, otherwise the inline inspection system will not be able to inspect any components.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.9.3 Teaching In References

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
You can check on the *References* tab in the *Inspections* window whether all the references in an inspection have been correctly taught in. Failure of any of the lights in the T. column to come on indicates that the corresponding reference was not taught in. If the lamp is red, a setting is wrong. Green indicates successful teach-in; the reference can also be confirmed (except for special mode) with **Quick teach**.  
70
6. Finish by starting an inspection run to verify the teach-in run. To do this, in Run options
,  Operation mode, select **Inspection run** from the list.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.9.3 Teaching In References

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Contour detection can be used when normal glue sampling is not possible (e.g. in curves with narrow radii). Every glue can be detected if the edges are recognizable in the image. In this case, sampling points are not the only checking method used, rather an entire search area is defined to search for the edges of the glue.  
To perform the relevant inspection, double-click and open the *Contour name* tab.  
1. Select an inspection and then select the vertical *Contours* tab on the left-hand side of the
screen.
55
2. Use the Image navigator
to go to the position where the polygon is to be created.
3. Click Add to create a new contour.
4. Double-clicking the contour opens the *Contour name* window.
5. Now click Add to add a polygon. 6. Mark this and click **Edit polygon points** to start the polygon editor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.10 Contour Detection

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
5. Now click Add to add a polygon. 6. Mark this and click **Edit polygon points** to start the polygon editor.
Actions in the editor are carried out with the left or right mouse button and by dragging the polygons with the mouse. Actions in the contour screen are carried out in the same way as for the areas. Contours can be added, copied, stored and deleted. As a general rule, it is also important here to ensure that a start position in ms / mm is defined for each contour. In practice, the required position is selected via the navigation and the polygon editor is then started.  
In this case, the start position is transferred from the navigation to the basic settings and the editor shows the respective section. The polygons can now be created and the associated glue and error definitions can be set.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.4.10 Contour Detection

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Polygons can be created interactively with the mouse. Editor tips are provided on the righthand side of the screen that simplify operation. When a polygon is selected, the respective search area is highlighted.  
The direction of the arrow should match the direction of impact of the robot. The contour must not be placed directly at the end or beginning of the bead.  
When the polygon has been created, the recognized errors and warnings are immediately listed in the error list. To prevent errors, the glue or error definitions may have to be adjusted in the *Polygon name* dialog. To do this, double-click the polygon to open it.  
All created polygons are listed in the polygon list. A contour model may contain one or more polygons. A polygon contains the search area and the glue and error definitions.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Define the search area with the polygon so that the glue bead and an adequate edge area are included. If the polygon was created with an edge area that is too small (too precisely on the glue bead), this could result in an error message, as the system cannot recognize any glue because the associated edge definition defined here is not within the search area.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Inspections

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Reflection filter The reflection filter can be used to minimize reflections on the glue bead and the sheet metal. The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be. With a value of Off, no reflections are eliminated; the filter is not active. Basic settings Click **Polygon active** to switch on contour detection at the respective image position. It is also possible to activate or deactivate several polygons in one image. To do this, select the required polygon and then click **Settings**. The *Polygon name* dialog opens where the polygon can be activated and deactivated in turn via the basic settings. If a polygon is inactive, the glue and error definitions cannot be changed. You will find further information on this in:
103
141
Search parameters Global reparameterization

text: Manage servers In the case of dual systems, two clients and two servers can be used. Or if the server is not located locally, but installed on the production computer, then it has to be created. To enable the correct link here, the servers are created with their IP address at this point. The function is active only when the default connection between server (localhost) and client is disconnected. The link is then subsequently established again in the client, with the candidates listed for selection in the main toolbar on the client under **Server**.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: About Shows Information About The Program Version. 4.6 Inconsistency Check
text:
If a parameter setting has been changed, an inconsistency check is performed in configuration mode when the menu commands File | Local application | Save, File | Local application | **Save as ...** and **File | External application | Transfer**  are executed. And of course also if the application is saved with the Save or Transmit buttons.  
The inspection system performs the following checks for this inconsistency check:  
· Was the sensor in the inspection(s) selected correctly? · Was the calibration in the inspection(s) selected correctly?
· Was the selected calibration valid? · Has the product ID already been used several times. · Has the inspection length been taught in for all the inspections created? Is the teach-in
process complete?
· Have all the areas and references created been taught in?
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > About Shows Information About The Program Version. 4.6 Inconsistency Check

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: About Shows Information About The Program Version. 4.6 Inconsistency Check
text:
process complete?
· Have all the areas and references created been taught in?
If at least one of these checks returns a negative result, a dialog opens showing which check was unsuccessful.
If the data is saved despite this warning, this may result in a system error (ERROR) during the next inspection run.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > About Shows Information About The Program Version. 4.6 Inconsistency Check

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
This functionality allows previously parameterized elements to be reparameterized at three different levels. At the product level, for example, one or more relevant products can be selected and the Settings button then activated to open a dialog that analyzes the selected products for the number of areas, references and polygons. Similarly, inspections of a product can also be parameterized collectively in one step. Even at the area level, reparameterization of the areas, references and/or polygons is possible. As soon as multiple marked list entries of areas, references or polygons are present and **Settings** is selected, reparameterization is activated with *Change error definitions*.  
If, for example, a value for the glue gap is to be changed generally for a left and a right door, proceed as follows:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.7 Global Reparameterization

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
If, for example, a value for the glue gap is to be changed generally for a left and a right door, proceed as follows:  
1. Mark the products that you want to reparameterize, because they use identical or similar parameters.
2. Open the **Change error definitions** dialog by selecting Settings
The system automatically determines the number of areas, references and polygons already created for the selected element. The parameters that can be set for areas, references and polygons can be changed using the buttons.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.7 Global Reparameterization

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Note - Global adoption of the new values The system does not check for the existing values of the parameters within the located areas, so that when a change is made, even individual limit values that are set differently are overwritten with the new value. Thus, individual single parameterizations are changed as well. Global reparameterization should therefore be carried out with caution.  
The core parameters that can be changed globally for areas, references and/or polygons are as follows:  
Areas
References
Polygons
Glue gap
Edge deviation
Glue gap
Glue position
Distance deviation
Glue position
Glue width
(depends on reference type)
Glue width
Checking method Glue detection
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.7 Global Reparameterization

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Distance deviation
Glue position
Glue width
(depends on reference type)
Glue width
Checking method Glue detection  
In order to retain individual settings, it is recommended to only use reparameterization in this way at a lower level. At the inspection level, you can specifically select the inspection for which you want to change the applicable areas, references or any existing polygons. A more selective approach would be possible at the area level or at a lower level with references and polygons.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.7 Global Reparameterization

text: The required sensor must be assigned to the product in configuration mode. For this assignment, the sensor must be created in the RTVision.t client. Launch sensor management by selecting **Manage sensors** in the **Settings** menu in configuration mode.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8 Sensor Management Launching Sensor Management In Configuration Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 4.8 Sensor Management Launching Sensor Management In Configuration Mode
text: Background information: There must be a connection to the RTVision.t server. The link to the sensor and thus also its calibration data are saved in the sensor management on the server side. During commissioning by the authorized service provider, this calibration data is configured via the **Sensor link** checkbox. This data applies universally, but can be changed in the RTVision.t client per product if necessary. To enable this, assignment of the sensor for the product is required and therefore product-specific sensor management maintained in the RTVision.t client. Note Sensor calibration is carried out at the factory, saved on the sensor and generally does not need to be replaced manually
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8 Sensor Management Launching Sensor Management In Configuration Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The RTVision.t system offers different variants depending on the local installation situation, application system and task. Many different sensor types are available, which vary, for example, as regards lighting parameters, filters and working distances.  
The default situation for the RTVision.t system is inspection with just ONE suitable sensor.  
However, the application can be configured and set up to use several sensors. The sensor manager of the application allows you to create several physical sensors and also virtual sensors that differ, for example, only in their brightness. The different sensors can then be assigned to the individual products via the RTVision.t client. If several physical sensors are in use, two conditions must be observed:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.1 System Variants

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
- the imaging system communicates with just ONE higher level controller - the sensors can only carry out inspections one at a time and not simultaneously or
parallel to each other.
Within the application you can define which brightnesses are to be used for the lighting of each sensor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.1 System Variants

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The sensor optics may only be installed and configured by the authorized service provider. The authorized service provider works on the RTVision.t server under Service | Hardware state and **Service | Sensor settings**. Leave these basic settings on the RTVision.t server to the authorized service provider since empirical values are required for the optimized setting of hardware, especially the sensors.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Note - Material Damage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The sensor systems delivered ex works (camera with strobe controller) always have a fixed IP address that is identical for all sensor optics. In addition, each sensor leaves the manufacturer with its factory calibration. Recalibration is usually not necessary. The strobe controller and cameras are then initialized in the RTVision.t server under Service | Hardware state and **Service | Sensor settings**. Only then can the sensor management in the  RTVision.tclient be used for the productspecific assignment.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Note - Material Damage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
What happens if you work with more than 1 sensor? If your system works with more than one physical sensor, the cameras and strobe controller must have different IP addresses. This is necessary so that they can be controlled selectively by the higher level controller and data transfer and evaluation is possible.  
- When the strobe controller and camera have been connected to the local network, the
sensor camera is assigned a new IP address with the installed configuration software (the software name depends on the type of camera).
- The associated strobe controller is then configured via the auxiliary program
ScLibClientQUISS\ScLibSDK\bin\Win64).
- The Find button is used to search for the strobe controller and, if necessary, it is assigned a
new IP address.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Sensor Management

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
- The Find button is used to search for the strobe controller and, if necessary, it is assigned a
new IP address.
Tip To simplify the initialization of several sensors in RTVision.t, it is advisable to connect and initialize just one system consisting of camera and strobe controller at a time. The assignment of the serial number (of the camera) and sensor in RTVision.t are thus unique.  
- RTVision.t server is started. - The basic settings are made in **Service | Sensor settings** in the **Adjust sensor** dialog. - Delay and pulse duration are filled by default. - Leave optimum output voltage activated. - Adjust the power supply for the lighting intensity and color.
Depending on the ideal light output, the ideal power supply must be selected. Empirical values and images to be tested after initialization show how well the power supply is adjusted.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Sensor Management

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Take advantage of the support offered by the authorized service partner to avoid incorrect settings and thus system damage or restrictions.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Sensor Management

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: With the **RGB sensor**, three mA fields are available for color selection; with the single color sensor, only one field **Current**  has to be set. Depending on the configuration of the sensor regarding scattered light or light with a polarized filter, base material and glue color, values between 500 mA and 1500 mA should be tried out here. The maximum values for power supply defined in the hardware must be observed to prevent overheating, for example. If white light is to be used with the RGB sensor, the same mA value (e.g. 1000mA) can be entered in all current channels; here, too, the overall maximum value for the strobe controller must be observed. These mA values can be optimized later and are not fixed in the first step.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Note - Material Damage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Tip The settings for the strobe controller in the RTVision.t server apply globally across all products. Special case of RGB sensor: For each individual product it is possible to optimize the strobe controller; this is then done for each product in configuration mode on the client. If the strobe controller is not activated for the product, the global server setting applies, which is often to be preferred.  
- Click Save data to apply and initialize the settings for the strobe controller. If initialization is
successful, the field is green.
You will find further information on this in:

server. This means that the RGB sensor can be optimized via the strobe controller individually for each product by means of the client. However, this setting also slows down the system, so this application should only be used in individual cases.
