chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Depending on the use and materials deployed, the RGB sensor provides flexibility for various products to define the lighting with ideal light-dark contrasts.  
With three color channels for blue, red and green, the RGB sensor can be adapted for every product and its specific properties. By combining the color channels, it can also be used for white. The lighting is controlled by an LED light controller (strobe controller) that addresses all three channels selectively to control the coloration. In the RTVision.t software, the strobe controller can be configured on the server and on the client (product-specific in this case).  
145
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.2 Rgb Sensor Settings

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
145
Global setting On the RTVision.t server, when the strobe controller is initialized in Service | Sensor settings, the power supply for optimum light-dark contrast is specified globally for all products. See details in section System variants Selective setting of the RGB sensor per product On the  RTVision.t client, you can specify a separate color definition of the strobe controller for each product. However, this applies only to this product and overwrites the global setting on the server only for this product. The other products remain covered by the global definition.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.2 Rgb Sensor Settings

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
In automatic mode, the strobe controller values of the RGB sensor configured for each product in configuration mode (Client) overwrite the global settings made on the server. This means that the RGB sensor can be optimized via the strobe controller individually for each product by means of the client, though this can adversely affect the testing process and possibly slow it down. For this reason, this feature of the product-specific setting should only be used for individual products.  
If the strobe controller on the client is not activated (not checked), the global settings defined on the server for the RGB sensor are used.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.2 Rgb Sensor Settings

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The GLOBAL setting (from the server) overwrites the product setting if the strobe controller on the product level on the client is NOT activated (see below).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Configuration Mode Description / Sensor Management

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Sensor calibration is carried out in the factory and generally does not have to be done again by you. Every sensor is calibrated in the factory and includes its own specific calibration data. This is entered in the application via the sensor link. In the event of faulty calibration, it is imperative that you contact the service partner.  
260
The calibration data for the sensor is loaded into the application in the *Adjust sensor* window.  
See: Replacing the sensor If the client still did not have access to the sensor and camera data (prior to commissioning), click the checkbox **Sensor link**.  
The stored data is transferred automatically to the client and the serial numbers of the sensor camera, including the calibration data, are loaded (green).  
154
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3 Sensor Dialog Note

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
154
Successful upload of the calibration is indicated when **Identical calibration** is green; the serial number of the sensor camera is also shown.  
It must be ensured that the calibration data of the new sensor is uploaded successfully. Only in absolutely exceptional cases should the relevant sensor be calibrated manually. This function is active only if you uncheck **Sensor link**. However, this replaces the specific calibration of the sensor installed at the factory with another calibration not stored on the sensor!! The individual steps involved in manual calibration are described in section Performing a calibration
.  
143
Open the sensor dialog In the Sensor management of the client, select a sensor or create a new one. Then click Adjust or double-click the required sensor in the list of sensors. The *Adjust sensor* dialog opens.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3 Sensor Dialog Note

section: Exposure Time Sets The Exposure Time In Percent.
text:
Calibration state Green indicates that the calibration is valid for the application.
It does not show whether the new calibration was already loaded following a sensor replacement. This is shown on the left-hand side of the screen under Identical calibration.  
The properties of the calibration can be displayed in Properties. OK

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
RTVision.t performs a visual inspection of the glue application during the application process. This inspection is based on the images supplied by the three cameras. The image information is shown in pixels. To make entering information as easy as possible for the user, the software uses millimeters instead of pixels. Before being delivered, the sensors are calibrated in the factory to establish the relationship between pixels and millimeters. Generally, end users do not have to carry out any manual calibration.  
Note - Material damage Sensor calibration is carried out in the factory and generally does not have to be done again by you. Every sensor already contains its own specific calibration data on delivery. This is entered in the application via the sensor link.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3.1 Calibration

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
CAUTION: Additional manual calibration overwrites the calibration values set in the factory.  Should this be unavoidable, it is imperative to consult the authorized service provider beforehand. The manufacturer rejects all liability claims in the event of sensors being calibrated independently.  
Calibration also determines the position of the three cameras relative to each other in order to identify the precise switching point and the overlap area. Calibration is performed using a calibration plate with a special pattern of dots. You will find further information on calibration in Performing a calibration
.  
154
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3.1 Calibration

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Note - AVOID performing a manual calibration !!! End users do not usually have to carry out any calibration manually. The calibration data is linked to the sensor technology and calibration is carried out in the factory.  
Before **doing anything,** contact the authorized service provider if you plan to carry out **manual calibration** yourself. The manufacturer shall not be liable for any kind of damage that occurs as a result of a manual calibration being performed independently.  
The following steps are required to perform a manual calibration:  
143
1.
In the Sensor management
in **Settings | Manage sensors**, first select a sensor or
create a new one. Then click **Settings**. 2.
An image of the calibration plate is required for automatic calibration. This can either be
loaded from the hard disk or recorded using the cameras.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3.2 Performing A Calibration Manually

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
loaded from the hard disk or recorded using the cameras.
If a new image is to be used for calibration, move the sensor to the calibration plate using the robot, or move the calibration plate under the sensor if the glue nozzle is stationary or use the mobile calibration device. The working distance of the sensor must be observed. The calibration plate and sensor must also be parallel to each other (no tilting). Then align the sensor with the calibration plate for positioning the camera. This is done by swiveling the sensor through its fixing axis or by swiveling the calibration plate. The position is correct when the dot outside the circle is visible in the top camera image and the dot inside the circle is visible in the bottom camera image and these two dots are as close as possible to the same vertical line (see screenshot below).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3.2 Performing A Calibration Manually

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
A good and cleanly executed calibration is a prerequisite for using the image processing system. Make sure to use the calibration stored on the sensor in the factory and avoid manual calibration where possible. Do not detach the sensor link following commissioning.  
You can load the calibration data for a newly replaced sensor without difficulty if a sensor link exists by selecting **Replace sensor**.  
To load an image from the hard disk, click **Load image**.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.8.3.2 Performing A Calibration Manually

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
· Calibration is performed automatically. · After successful calibration, the **Valid calibration** lamp is lit.
4. To reconstruct the calibration later, click the **Save image**  button to save the image used for the calibration.  
If calibration was not successful, click the  **Configuration** button for an advanced setting option.  
The simplest method is to optimize the point search by switching to manual segmenting and additionally adjusting the brightness threshold.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 3. Click **Sensor Link**.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Note - Danger Of Confusing Calibration And Referencing
text: While the manual calibration procedure is identical to the reference configuration process, a valid calibration must already exist for the reference configuration. Do NOT change the calibration of your sensor from its ex works setting in the course of the reference configuration for the subsequent target checks. The target check only looks for potential deviations from the reference within the system, comprising sensor, gluing unit, nozzle and robot. It does NOT calibrate the sensor and also should NOT result in manual calibration.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Note - Danger Of Confusing Calibration And Referencing

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Default values for new areas, references and polygons can be changed in the RTVision.t client via the menu entry Settings | Default values
. The input dialogs are the same as the dialogs used to set the parameters for the individual areas, references and polygons. Default values are to be understood as initial values or as a basic data record for parameterization. Control elements, such as basic settings, for which defining default values makes no sense, are however locked or not included. If any default values are changed, these changes relate only to areas, references and polygons **created** from that point onwards.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.9 Default Values

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: For this reason, the appropriate default record is assigned to the higher-level inspection. The inspection is opened for this purpose and the correct default record from the selection menu is assigned under **General**. Once this has been done, this corresponding data record is assigned as the initial value for further parameterization for all new areas, references and polygons to be created. When the area is created interactively (from the image sequence by marking with the mouse and selecting **Create area** in the context menu), some parameters in the default data record are already assigned the optimal values. This means that some default values are already overwritten directly when the new area is created, since the values that can be optimized directly are always set. The contents of existing areas/references/polygons can therefore no longer be changed via a new default link
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.9 Default Values

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . The contents of existing areas/references/polygons can therefore no longer be changed via a new default link. It is only when new ones are created in turn that a modified link would take effect in the inspection.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.9 Default Values

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Default values simplify and accelerate parameterization significantly because they present fixed basic data, which is an ideal functionality in large projects. Before all areas within the inspections are created, the fixed parameters for the glue can therefore be defined, for example if error definitions are generally always identical for an inspection or also for the product. This error definition is already transferred for each new area created when entered as a default. Note that changes to default values do not impact existing areas, references and polygons.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 4.9 Default Values

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: It is recommended to create your own default data records when inspecting certain bead types, which can work with a standardized parameterization for the entire inspection. On the client, select **Settings | Default values** and create an initial data record Default_1 with Add.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Creating Default Data Records

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Control Elements For Creating And Defining Default Data Records
text:
Add To create a new data record, click Add. A new entry with a default name appears in the default list.  
Copy Use this button if you want to duplicate a data record. The copied data record is initially given a default name (this can be changed).  
Delete To delete a data record, first select the required data record in the default list. Then click Delete. Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes.  
Rename To rename a data record, first select the entry in the default list. Then click **Rename**. Change the name as required.

text: Default By selecting this option, the marked default data record is identified as a favorite. If no specific assignment is intended, this favorite is used by default for the inspection. It is only if no data record at all is available that the selection list for default values remains empty; the RTVision.t system uses the default values defined in the system code. Manual parameterization can also be carried out successfully in this case. chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
RTVision.t can be delivered in several language versions. The basic languages are German and English. If you are missing languages, please contact the service partner. The language can be switched at any time during operation, though this operation is then password protected. It is not necessary to restart the program.  
To switch to another language, select the required language version from the Language menu. The language is switched separately within each RTVision.t module or can also be set at a higher level. You will find further information on this in: Special unlicensed specifications 246

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 5 Automatic Mode 5 Automatic Mode 5.1 Views
text:
The live view shows the course of the sampling point on the glue bead in-process during image recording from the camera's point of view (left). On the error map, the glue bead movement can be followed live in parallel (center). On the right-hand side, as usual, the information displayed includes simple statistics of the previous results, the current operation mode, the product being tested at the moment and, after the inspection run, the up-to-date result.  
Tip - Frame sizes are variable You can adjust the size of the individual screen areas with respect to each other using the left mouse button. This change remains as long as the RTVision.t server application is running. The frame that can be changed is displayed by changing the mouse cursor to this icon
.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 5 Automatic Mode 5 Automatic Mode 5.1 Views

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 5 Automatic Mode 5 Automatic Mode 5.1 Views
text:
.  
To display the results, the system automatically switches to the error view (if preset). If there is no error, only the statistics and the error map are displayed here. If the result is WARN or NOK, further error details including a camera view of the error sequence are displayed depending on the default setting. If automatic switching between live view and error view is deactivated, display mode can be set permanently by clicking with the mouse. The error map can be displayed here throughout the entire inspection run, for example, with Overview.  
The error map shows the errors found on a component during the inspection. This involves storing a component image in any format (e.g. digital photo, CAD drawing in the file formats *.bmp, *.jpg or *.png) for each product. A black area appears if there is no component image stored for a product.

section: Simple Statistic / Trend Indicator / Reference And Result View
text:
Current results and the status of the inspection run (RUN) are displayed here and recorded statistically.  
The following results are possible:  
| Result                               | Display   |
|--------------------------------------|-----------|
| Background                           | Meaning   |
| OK                                   | Green     |
| OK                                   |           |
| Warning                              | WARN      |
| but the warning limits were exceeded |           |
| NOK                                  | Red       |
| OK                                   |           |
| System error                         | ERR       |
| triggered by a hardware fault.       |           |  
In addition, the trend indicator provides support for assessing the results. It indicates negative developments at an early stage in the course of the quality checks.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
If the inspection system finds deviations from the set error and warning tolerances in the glue application while inspecting a component, it outputs an NOK or warning.  
169
In automatic mode, apart from the control elements described in the section Control elements and displays in automatic mode the following information is also provided:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.3 Result Display In Automatic Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The current result of the last inspection run is always displayed. This can be OK, WARN, NOK and ERR for system errors. The latter is usually due to a hardware error. The remaining three results, on the other hand, refer to the inspection task. Compliance with or deviation from the set error and warning tolerances in the glue application for the respective component is output. Even one image recording of the image sequence with a NOK rating generates an NOK in the quality inspection for the entire inspection run. The number of inspected components of a product / component type is indicated with Total, if the list entry is selected accordingly or is set to automatic.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Simple Statistics

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Automatic Mode / Result Display In Automatic Mode
text: The result display for the simple statistics can be varied in the drop-down menu below via the list entries Automatic, Total and Product_x. The result is assigned to the currently inspected component of the type Product_x with the list entry **Automatic**. With **Total**, the results are displayed as the total of all components inspected so far. Alternatively, the display can be selected **for the specific product**. The **Reset** button resets the display of the simple statistics to zero, regardless of the list entry selected. The results determined up to the reset are not deleted, rather only the display of all products is reset to zero. This action cannot be undone. However, the reset is performed independently of the selection.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Automatic Mode / Result Display In Automatic Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Automatic Mode / Result Display In Automatic Mode
text:
The information on the operation mode and product name listed below always reflects the current reference and is likewise independent of the display mode selected for the simple statistics. The **Statistic** dialog opens by double-clicking the simple statistic. It displays the detailed statistics data in tabular form and has to be updated manually by clicking **Update**. The individual results of the various inspections are also displayed in tabular form when the desired product is selected. The display always refers to the date of the last reset (status line on the left). Clicking Settings opens the dialog of the same name: The Statistic view and Trend indicator are two independent settings that refer to the "simple statistic" in automatic mode.  
Statistic view
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Automatic Mode / Result Display In Automatic Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Automatic Mode / Result Display In Automatic Mode
text:
Statistic view
defines the number of days, weeks or months to be displayed retrospectively. Note that the statistics data always uses the time of the last reset as the start of the counter. In addition, 1 day is defined as the current day, i.e. "today". The data in the time window for yesterday and today is defined by entering "2".
Trend indicator
can be defined as a percentage of the number of inspected components for the current product. The maximum possible reference number is 100 parts. A distinction is made between Constant in a certain % range, Slight or Strong increment for WARN, NOK or ERR. Three differently colored and aligned arrow icons are used for display purposes.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Automatic Mode / Result Display In Automatic Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The error list shows the individual errors found during the last faulty inspection. The faulty image recording is also replicated on the bead both on the error map and in the camera view. The errors found (ERROR and NOK) are described in plain text in the error information (left). In addition, the reference values for the taught-in and actual values of the glue bead just inspected are shown. Clicking the errors in the list shows the associated error image, the position of the error on the component on the error map and the reference / actual values. In the event of a system error (ERR), the cause of the error is specified here. Errors  that belong together are grouped, and the errors are also allocated to the individual inspections.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Error List

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The automatic switch between live view and error view can be activated / deactivated with this button. When activated, the system automatically switches from live view (during the inspection) to error view (with fade-in of the result) during each inspection run. If the result is OK, the window that normally represents the error view remains empty because there is no error. The error map is always part of each overview, regardless of the result and the selected display mode.  
56
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Display Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
56
If it is deactivated, a display mode of your choice can be set permanently with a mouse click. The overview is advantageous here, as it shows the error map in large size. In the event of a NOK, all detected warnings and errors are marked on the map with an orange or red dot. In order to ensure a synchronous display of results, the positions must be set up correctly under Configuring the product image and bead paths
.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Display Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
This function displays the statistics table with the product-related result data below the image views. An overall result is also displayed if multiple products within an application file are created on one inspection system. All OKs, warnings, NOKs and system errors are listed separately for each product result and overall result. By selecting a product, the dialog described above regarding detailed statistics can be opened. Automatic mode is not interrupted with these actions.  
.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Show / Hide Detailed Statistics

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
278
By displaying error symbols on the error map, the corresponding location on the component is additionally marked. The typical error symbols and their meaning are shown under Overview of error icons
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Show / Hide Error Details

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The display type of the camera images is preset here. It is possible to switch between the single camera views and the round view, i.e. the 360° view. The sampling point can be observed in both views in live mode. The images transferred from the sensor cameras are shown next to the error map. The live images can be seen there during an inspection run. The five sampling points are also shown in the camera image in which the glue bead can be seen. The number of sampling points can be recognized by the number of green lines - five is the default setting. The blue direction indicates the search direction for the component-glue-component transitions. The yellow line stands for the width calculation at this point, incl. display of the position detection (important in curves).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Switching The Camera View

Communication This button opens the Communication dialog between the RTVision.t server and the higher-level controller. Hardware state Allows the current hardware state to be queried and renewed connection to the current hardware configuration to be established in cases where a sensor is replaced or a different hardware problem arises. If hardware and/or communication problems arise, these are displayed in red in the server (automatic mode) window on the right-hand side below the manufacturer logo. This display is not updated automatically however and therefore indicates a hardware error in general without stating any details. With **Service | Hardware state** (password-protected), the cause of the error can be located or ideally resolved with the buttons **Update hardware status** and **Reconnect the hardware**.

Opens the dialog for setting the strobe controller. The sensor lighting can be controlled globally on the basis of different current values [mA].  
The setting is then checked with an image recording.  
The settings made here in the server for the sensor impact all products globally for which no assignment exists (to be performed in the client).

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Automatic Mode / Menu Commands In Automatic Mode
text:
The dialog above shows the setting options for customers using the **RGB sensor**, which offers three colors for parameterization. Via the Current settings for the three channels Current 1 (blue), **Current 2 (green)** and Current 3 (red), you can control the color of the lighting that is to be used globally. In configuration mode, the strobe controller can be set differently from the global setting for each individual product. If a separate setting is not defined on the client for the strobe controller, the basic setting executed globally on the server and typically made by the authorized service provider is used for all products.  
247

.

Choose the period for which you want to zip all your data and the results data. Then click Export  to export the compressed file and send it authorized service partner. While you are selecting the data, the size of the zip file is shown; consider the size that you can send by e-mail or FTP. If necessary, reduce the amount of data over a reduced period of time and send multiple packets to the service department. You will find further information on this in:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Choosing **View | Communication** opens the communications dialog between the RTVision.t
(server) and the controls. It can be used to check whether the bus connection is active. In the dialog shown below, the connection to the higher-level controller has been correctly established. The control lamp is active (green), the system indicates active communication. The **All bytes** tab shows the inputs and outputs as numerical values. The display can be toggled between the following types:  
· Binary: binary display, e.g. "00000100"
· Decimal: decimal display, e.g. "005"
· Hexadecimal:  hexadecimal display, e.g. "05"
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.5 Communication Dialog

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The second tab "Base I/O" shows the current states of the inputs and outputs of the basic interface within the context of the RTVision.tlog. The third tab, **Repair I/O**, is only visible if the repair function in the software is activated and the corresponding signal inputs / outputs are defined. The dialog shows the inputs and outputs required for the repair.
Note - Licensed repair function The repair function is an optional enhancement of RTVision.t and must be enabled separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-level controller must also be extended.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Automatic Mode / Communication Dialog

text:
Note - Interface extensions An additional tab in the communications dialog shows supplementary custom interface extensions in relation to communication, for example. This tab is usually referred to as Optional I/O. It is used, for example, when a link to the glue controller software is envisaged. But also in the case of data transfers via the extended interface (e.g. seam numbers).

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Clear all Clears the display of all logbook entries except for the last current entry. The display allows up to 1000 entries; if the display is deleted, the content remains stored in the logbook file, only the display has been cleaned. Group Selects the logbook entries that are to be displayed according to the categories All groups, System, Inspection or *Communication*.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Control Elements In The Logbook

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Min. Priority Selects The Priority Level For Displaying The Logbook Entries. Each Level Is Displayed With "Green", While Normal Messages Are Filtered With "Yellow", Which Are To Be Assigned To This And The Red Level. With "Red", Only Messages Marked In Red That Show Errors Are Displayed.
text: Last messages Is checked by default and limits the focus to the most recent logbook entry. This is particularly beneficial in automatic mode. You can deselect this button if you want to browse older logbook entries. Entries already deleted in the display are still in the log file and can be viewed there. Display signals This function outputs additional messages that relate to the signal communication between the higher-level system and RTVision.t . Signals such as "Start inspection" and "Stop inspection" are logged here for each call associated with a signal transfer. This fills the log file within a very short time and should therefore only be activated during commissioning. Deactivation is recommended in normal production mode.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Min. Priority Selects The Priority Level For Displaying The Logbook Entries. Each Level Is Displayed With "Green", While Normal Messages Are Filtered With "Yellow", Which Are To Be Assigned To This And The Red Level. With "Red", Only Messages Marked In Red That Show Errors Are Displayed.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Close Closes The Logbook. View | Display History
text: Opens the history in which the product-specific results are output. The results OK / NOK / ERR and WARN are listed together with the product name, date and time stamp, operation mode and acquisition status. The "Acquire state" column is only filled if the storage status of an image sequence is also captured when it is recorded. Regardless of the result, only the completeness of the saved image sequence is displayed in the last column "Acquire state." Complete stands for the complete closure of the saved image sequence. An error occurs, for example, when there is insufficient memory or other reasons exist for a "component not inspected". By doubleclicking the respective line, the corresponding detailed information can be called up in the viewer that opens.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Close Closes The Logbook. View | Display History

section: Update Refresh Instigates A Manual Update Of The Display In Table Format, Whereas The Simple Statistics Are Automatically Updated With Each Inspection Run.
text: Settings Select the required product with the associated inspection from the list. Use Settings to open the dialog of the same name, in which you can specify a time window with total duration in the past via the Statistic view. Time window options include All or 1 day(s) or week(s) or **month(s)**. 1 day means the current day (today), 2 days means, for example, today and yesterday. This not only defines the display of results in the detailed statistics dialog, but also influences the view of the simple (automatically updating) statistics in the server dialog (automatic mode). Please note that the settings for the statistic view and the trend indicator are independent of each other. In addition, the time at which the display was reset is decisive for the renewed display in the statistics. It is the starting point for recalculating the display values

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
To be able to parameterize the system, you must start by recording image sequences. This is done in automatic mode. To make the configuration possible, the products must be created beforehand.  
177
1. In automatic mode in the menu bar
first click **Settings | Record image sequence**.
Automatic mode will not be exited while the recorded sequences are being configured.
2. Authorize yourself by entering a password in the *Login* dialog.
3. Select the relevant product in the *Record images* window.
4. Now click the **Configure image recording** button or double-click the relevant product. 5. Enter the number of required image sequence recordings directly in the numerical field in
the *Configure image recording* window or increase the number using the arrow buttons.
Click OK to confirm.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.7 Recording Image Sequences

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
the *Configure image recording* window or increase the number using the arrow buttons.
Click OK to confirm.
Long glue beads are automatically divided into multiple image sequences. Multiple passes are required in this case. In each of these passes, the inspection system attempts to record as many inspections as possible. At a minimum, however, one inspection per image sequence will be recorded. This means that the product and number of inspections must already be known and created for the configuration.  
The record counter is only reset when all the inspections selected in the Record images dialog have been recorded.  
6. Optionally, you can select the inspections to be recorded. To do this, click the Select
individual inspections button.
Interpretation of figure: If all inspections are selected, all three associated image sequences are recorded and saved in two successive cycles.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.7 Recording Image Sequences

text:
7. Click OK to finish configuring the recording.
If the higher-level controller now sends the start signal for a component previously selected for image sequence recording to the inspection system, recording of the glue bead will start. The current status of image recording is shown when a recording is currently taking place or has taken place.

text:
The status referred to above for these image sequences is shown in the **History** dialog in the last column **Acquire state**. The recorded images are saved on the hard disk as soon as image recording ends.  
If the inspection system is to be parameterized using the newly recorded images, you will need to switch to configuration mode.  
Tip - Recording image sequences The function can be selected both in the server and in the client. However, both counter and execution are always handled by the server, since the image recording initiated on the client must be transferred to the server prior to execution.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Saving the result data on the server makes it possible to save not just error data but all the data recorded by the system during a component inspection to an individual XML file (one XML file is created per component). This server setting applies globally.  
Note - Required for detailed view in the viewer This setting is mandatory if the viewer is to be used fully to analyze past results. This default setting is required for the detailed view in the viewer to provide the necessary data.  
Results data can also be changed and saved for each product, with the frequency indicating which component result (of a product) will be saved.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.8 Saving Result Data

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
For example, "10" means that the data of every tenth component of the same product is saved. The frequency can be set per product on the client, whereas the setting is globally valid in automatic mode (server). The storage interval on the server under **Settings | Save optional data** can be used to further reduce the volume of data. If different workpiece types (components/products) are produced and inspected in a station, in addition to activating result data storage generally in configuration mode (Client), you can decide whether the respective product should be excluded from the storage of result data. The following result data can be logged:  
91
· Width inspection (brightness measurement with activated Brightness analysis
)
· Position variances of the glue bead from references · Distance check using double and/or triple references
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.8 Saving Result Data

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
To globally activate the storage of results in automatic mode (server), the following steps are necessary:  
177
1. In automatic mode (server), in the menu bar
click **Settings | Save optional data**.
2. Authorize yourself by entering a password in the *Login* dialog. 3. Activate the *Save results* checkbox in the **Save optional data** window.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The user manager can be used to create users globally who are assigned a user level.  
Each user level is defined with fixed access rights by the system, distinguishing between
"Administrator", "User" and "Disabled". Access rights are not assigned individually to the levels, but are fixed. Choose Disabled to block the user. The advantage of this is that the user data is retained, but the user has no rights. Access can thus be activated and deactivated for a short time. Full access exists for the administrator level, while the user is often restricted with respect to global setting variants.  
Calling up the user manager To launch the user manager, select **Settings | Manage users** in the menu bar. To be able to use the user manager, you must have administrator rights in RTVision.t .
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.9 User Management

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Add To create a new user, click Add. A new entry with a default name appears in the list of users. The new user does not have a password yet. You can rename a user and change the user level by clicking the **Adjust** button. Delete To delete users, first select the required user(s) in the list of users. Then click **Delete**. Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes. The currently logged in user cannot be deleted and neither can the administrator, even after acknowledging the confirmation prompt.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Control Elements

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Rename To rename a user, select the required user in the list of users. Then click **Rename**. Change the name as required. As a logged-in user, you cannot change your own designation. Settings You can modify user properties here. This concerns the assignment of a user level and changing the password.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Automatic Mode / User Management

section: Arrow Up / Down The Arrows Can Be Used To Change The Order Of The Users In The List.
text:
Name To rename a user, first select the required user in the list of users. Then click **Settings**. You can edit the user name in the **User name** selection field
(same function as **Rename**). User level The level determines the rights of the individual users in RTVision.t . To change the level, start by selecting the required user in the user list. Then click **Settings**. You can then assign a level to this user in the **User level** selection field. Change password To set the password for a user, first select the required user. Then click **Settings** followed by Change password. You can enter the new password in the dialog shown below. The password must be entered twice as a precaution. Both entries must be the same, otherwise an error message will be shown and you will have to enter the password again.

The currently logged-in user can change his_her password directly via Settings | Change password. User management does not need to be called up for this purpose, unless the user is not at Administrator user level.  
OK Click OK to save actions / changes made and close the user manager. Cancel Click **Cancel** to discard actions/ changes made and close the user manager.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
RTVision.t can be delivered in several language versions. The basic languages are German and English. If you are missing languages, please contact the service partner. Integration of additional languages is possible, but must be ordered from the manufacturer as a language package. The languages can be changed at any time during operation, but the switching feature may be password protected. It is not necessary to restart the program.  
To switch to another language, select the required language version from the Language menu. The language is switched separately within each RTVision.t module or can also be set at a higher level.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.10 Switching Language

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: If the associated optional unlicensed specification is set up, the system can be preset so that all other modules operate in line with the language setting of the first module opened. The individual language switch per module is no longer necessary. In addition to the display of the interface in the local language, the names are assigned automatically when new products, inspections, areas, references and polygons are created in the set language. You will find further information on this in: Special unlicensed specifications 246
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 5.10 Switching Language

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 6 Results Evaluation 6 Results Evaluation 6.1 The Viewer
text:
The RTVision.t system logs all relevant result data in the background and saves it in special xml files on the server for further processing. This data can be loaded and displayed via these result files. The viewer is a useful enhancement to the system for presenting result data and its error evaluation in graphical form.  
When a component inspection has been completed, select **Display history** in the **View** main menu. The history opens. Double-click a row to open the **viewer**.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 6 Results Evaluation 6 Results Evaluation 6.1 The Viewer

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 6 Results Evaluation 6 Results Evaluation 6.1 The Viewer
text: The date, time, product name and result status are shown in the history. If the list is displayed via the server, double-clicking a line opens the viewer with the selected individual result of the product. The results are also automatically sorted by color. The RTVision.t viewer module can also be started as a standalone program and load the desired result report (xml files). By opening the viewer multiple times, individual reports can be viewed side by side at the same time.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 6 Results Evaluation 6 Results Evaluation 6.1 The Viewer

text:
Storage of result data must be activated as a prerequisite for viewing details in the RTVision.t viewer. On the RTVision.t server, activate the corresponding function under Settings | Save optional data.  
36
Tip With values of under 5 ms, all inspections will be recorded since the system records an image "only" every 5 ms by default (see also Image structure / image sequences
)
If the viewer is opened from the History, individual inspections can be selected. This activates the **Detailed view** button. Double-click to open the detailed view. Extensive graphical evaluation options are provided here, especially for analyzing errors.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Control Elements In The Detailed View
text:
Errors, warnings and calculated values can be displayed as detailed graphics. The product overview screen gives users a relatively precise idea of where the problem zones are; a detailed evaluation can then be carried out precisely via the inspection data calculated.  
Select an individual inspection and click **Detailed view** to evaluate the results further.  
Left-click the overview to show and hide individual windows, such as glue gap, glue width, glue offset, glue brightness or error rate. Separate criteria such as reference gap, reference offset or reference distance have also been set up for evaluating references.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Control Elements In The Detailed View

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: This is an example of a detailed view using a glue gap analysis as an example. The warning is displayed here as soon as it has been taught in as a tolerance, even if it was not output as a result. The point of the inspection where the transition of a warning to a fault is exceeded can be seen clearly here. When parameterized accordingly, warning and error could be detected as separate results. This precise error evaluation allows optimum parameterization of the system. For some interpretations, it helps to view the checking method applied at the same time as the associated error definitions of the inspection on the client. In the detailed view it is possible to enlarge individual areas by dragging the window with the left mouse button. Right-click to return to the original view. Various views can also be displayed via the buttons that can be selected above the view
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Results Evaluation / Detailed View

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . Various views can also be displayed via the buttons that can be selected above the view. The first view after opening always shows a view with all active buttons; if you select these afterwards, the displays change accordingly. Some parameters can be enabled / disabled by clicking the name above the view (e.g. error / warning).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Results Evaluation / Detailed View

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The inspection system RTVision.t must be able to communicate with the robot controller (hereinafter called the "controller") for the purpose of exchanging various signals and data. The signals are used, for example, to transmit inspection results, start / stop events or general system messages (see below for details); the image processing system is always the secondary system in this case. Communication with the controller takes place via fieldbus systems (Profibus, DeviceNet, CANopen) or Industrial Ethernet systems (Profinet, EtherNet/IP, EtherCAT). The basic interface can be adapted in terms of its communication with existing glue controllers and additional signals exchanged by extending the interface.  
Tip - Joint definition of interface Prior to installing the RTVision.t system, consult with manufacturer in order to define details of the data transfer jointly in advance.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 7 Communication 7 Communication 7.1 General

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The sequence of communication from start to finish for RTVision.t is as follows: Special case of "Change inspection": This signal is used in special cases for the uninterrupted transition from inspection_ x to inspection_x+1.  
These cases occur only when the bead to be monitored is exceptionally long and exceeds the max. number of images in the memory.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: A change from 0 to 1 indicates the start of a product, a change from 1 to 0 ends the current product.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 7.2.1 Incoming Signals Product On / Off (0.0)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: A change from 0 to 1 indicates the start of the inspection period, a change from 1 to 0 ends the current inspection area. Signal changes in this bit will be ignored if no product is selected (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Inspection On / Off (0.1)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Each signal edge (change from 0 to 1 or 1 to 0) during an inspection period (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 1 and "Inspection ON / OFF" is 1) causes an uninterruptible change to the next inspection. Signal edges outside of inspection periods (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0 or "Inspection ON / OFF" is 0) will be ignored. This function is required if inspection parameters, such as the exposure settings for the sensor, are to be changed without interruption during application of the glue or sealant.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Change Inspection (0.2)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The number to select the product (component) is an 8Bit unsigned integer. The number is read when a product is selected (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" from 0 to 1).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Product Id (1.0 - 1.7)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The system receives the speed of the robot as an unsigned integer via bits 2.0 to 3.7. A 0
(zero) corresponds to 0 mm/s and the value 65,535 ((2^16) - 1) corresponds to the maximum speed that can be transmitted. This can be configured in mm/s. The transmitted data must use the same multiplier that has also been stored in the system. Configuration takes place in the file "RTVision_t.ini". This file may only be processed explicitly by the authorized service provider. The assignment is linear.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Speed (2.0 - 3.7)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Mirrored signals The mirrored signals indicate which input signals are currently applied to the system.  
However, delays can occur between the input signal (e.g. input: Product ON / OFF à 1) and the mirror (e.g. output: Product ON / OFF à 1). These delays are caused by tasks the system has to perform first in order to achieve the required status. The following signals are mirrored: Product ON / OFF (0.0) Mirrors the incoming signal "Product ON / OFF". Inspection ON / OFF (0.1) Mirrors the incoming signal "Inspection ON / OFF". Change inspection (0.2) Mirrors the incoming signal "Change inspection". Product ID (1.0 - 1.7) Mirrors the incoming product ID. The product ID is read in at the moment the product is selected (Product ON / OFF à 1).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 7.2.2 Outgoing Signals

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The status messages provide information on the actual, current system status. There can also be delays between the input signal and the change in status with these signals due to tasks the system needs to perform.  
The following states can be reported:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Status Messages

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: System On / Off (2.0) Is Set To 1 When The Vision System Is Started. Automatic On / Off (2.1) Is Set To 1 When Automatic Mode Is Started. Product Selectable (2.2) Is Set To 1 When The System Is Expecting A Product To Be Selected. Inspection Selectable (2.3) Is Set To 1 When An Inspection Can Be Selected. Inspection Changeable (2.4) Is Set To 1 If An Inspection Can Be Changed. Result Available (2.5) Is Set To 1 If A Product Was Ended And Therefore A Result Is Available (Ok, Warn, Nok, Err).
text:
Hardware ready (2.6) The sensor, camera and strobe controller are checked by the system when the program is started, when the product is selected and also when the sensor is checked manually to determine if it is correct.  
Signal is set to 1 if the hardware status is OK, to zero if one or more components (also cable connection) is/are faulty.

Automatic On / Off (2.1) Is Set To 1 When Automatic Mode Is Started. Product Selectable (2.2) Is Set To 1 When The System Is Expecting A Product To Be Selected. Inspection Selectable (2.3) Is Set To 1 When An Inspection Can Be Selected. Inspection Changeable (2.4) Is Set To 1 If An Inspection Can Be Changed. Result Available (2.5) Is Set To 1 If A Product Was Ended And Therefore A Result Is Available (Ok, Warn, Nok, Err).

section: Result Messages: The System Uses The Result Messages To Report The Result Of The Most Recent Run.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 7.2.3 Programming The Controller Selecting A Product
text:
A product may only be selected by the controller if the following signals are set:
214
Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals
)
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1 2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1 2.2 "Product selectable" to 1
213
Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals
)
0.0 "Product ON / OFF" to 0
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0 Sequence on the controller side  
1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly. 2. Check whether the outgoing signals from the controller (see above) are set correctly. 3. Send product number to RTVision.t . 4. Set signal "Product ON / OFF" to 1
When the product has been selected, RTVision.t sets the following signals. 1.0-1.7 "Product ID" correspond to the bits of the product number 0.0 "Product ON/OFF" to 1 2.2 "Product selectable" to 0 2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 1
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 7.2.3 Programming The Controller Selecting A Product

"Inspection selectable" will be left at 0 at the time when "Product ON / OFF" is mirrored.

The controller may send the signal "Change inspection" only if the following signals are set:
214
Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals
)
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1 2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1 2.2 "Product selectable" to 0 2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0 2.4 "Inspection changeable" to 1
The first inspection of a product must not be started with the signal "Change inspection" (0.2); instead it must always be started by setting the signal "Inspection ON / OFF" (0.0) to 1 (from 0). The last inspection of a product must not be ended with the signal "Change inspection" (0.2); instead it must always be ended by setting the signal "Inspection ON / OFF" (0.0) to 0 (from 1). The signal "Change inspection" (0.2) may only be changed if the signal "Inspection ON / OFF" (0.0) is set to 1.

Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals
)
