chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: For the target check, the complete system and its individual components (from the robot gripper arm through the dispenser with nozzle to the sensor) are referenced against each other, i.e. an image recording of the reference target is optimized in configuration mode
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.1 Target Check: Configuration And Workflow

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: (Client) and saved as a reference value in the product or application. The application configured in this way with a specific product is transmitted to the server, where it is available for a repeat target check after a predefined product ID is retrieved. It checks the extent to which the current system status concerning imaging inspection deviates from a saved target status (reference). This is done using a permanently mounted reference target, which is approached when called. One possible application would be, for example, saving a cyclic call of the target check in the PLC so that the reference and actual position of the system with respect to the reference target can be determined.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.1 Target Check: Configuration And Workflow

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Furthermore, the test program can also be started after every mechanical replacement of a component (e.g. gripper, robot, sensor, nozzle, etc.) involving changes within the system and, if the result is NOK, the cause of the error can be determined and the magnitude of the error can be checked with regard to its relevance. The settings made are specific for each station, as they reference the robot, sensor, gripper arm and dispenser with nozzle together as an (overall) system.  
Note This test program is an optional function of the RTVision.t system and can be enabled after installation. Significant effort is required for the configuration and has to be clarified in advance before commissioning. The functional checkbox in the RTVision.t client is called **Compare sensor** at product level. If necessary, contact the authorized service partner.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.1 Target Check: Configuration And Workflow

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note: Configuration of a reference is NOT the same as sensor calibration. The latter is carried out in-house by the manufacturer and is permanently saved on the sensor upon delivery. Repeat calibration (manual) via RTVision.t is generally not required and is not intended. The manufacturer accepts no liability for any damage resulting from independently performed manual calibration of the sensor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.1 Target Check: Configuration And Workflow

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: For **configuration of the target check**, paths must be programmed (by the plant manufacturer) and at the same time settings made in the RTVision.tsystem. For configuration of the target check, a reference image of the reference target is saved for the current sensor. In the target checks that are then performed automatically, this reference is checked for current deviations of the nozzle position, which can be set in pixels. For better orientation, the conversion of the pixel data into millimeters is displayed in an information field, depending on the calibration of the sensor used.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.1 Target Check: Configuration And Workflow

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
· The reference target is mounted as close as possible to "Home" and must be
unchangeable for repeated approach of the industrial robot.
· The robot controller must be programmed so that when a predefined product ID is called,
the center of the nozzle is directed to the center of the reference target, with the nozzle positioned perpendicular to the plate.
· The working distance must be maintained with a deviation of +/- 1 mm and the center
points must be exactly above each other.
· The robot arm rotates the sensor head until the three cameras display an optimal image
of the reference target.
· Note this in the RTVision.t client in Settings | Manage sensors | Sensor | Settings
(see below). It is considered optimal if all three cameras find and display the circle points on top of each other.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Procedure - Robot Controller

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
(see below). It is considered optimal if all three cameras find and display the circle points on top of each other.
When the optimal position is reached, this position must be saved as a fixed target position before the reference target in the industrial robot.  
Note - Observe in the robot controller! The test program created is called in the RTVision.t server. The predefined product ID is called with the saved inspection. For communication, the following applies: product ON, inspection ON, a short wait time must be observed (e.g. <100 ms before the reference target), the signal for inspection OFF, product OFF then follows. The robot can then leave the position before the reference target. The result **OK / NOK** is output in automatic mode (server).  
For the initial configuration described here, additional settings in the RTVision.tclient are required.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Procedure - Robot Controller

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Procedure - Settings In The Rtvision.Tclient In Two Steps
text:
· Creation of the product with predefined ID (Client)
and
· creation of the reference image - configuration of a reference with subsequent
transmission of the application to the RTVision.tserver.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Procedure - Settings In The Rtvision.Tclient In Two Steps

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Step 1: Procedure - Creating A Product With Predefined Id
text:
· Start the RTVision.tserver and client. Select **Connect**. · In the client, create an application with a product, e.g. "reference target" to which you
assign the sensor and the defined product ID (in the example ID=99).
The target check is always linked with the product and its predefined ID via an inspection defined by the system. Accordingly, this ID may not be used for any other product inspection, as it does not allow individual configuration of the inspections.  
· Check the box for **Compare sensor**  and a specific system inspection is created
automatically. To do this, existing inspections must be deleted.
· When you confirm the confirmation prompt, an inspection is created by the system, which
cannot be edited by the user (and administrator).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Step 1: Procedure - Creating A Product With Predefined Id

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image
text:
Before the application is transmitted, the reference image is configured. The industrial robot is moved to the position "Target position" and the reference image is shown in the dialog in the RTVision.t client under **Automatic | Manage sensors | Sensor | Settings** as described above. The sensor can also be accessed via the main menu by selecting Settings | Manage sensors.  
Having linked the sensor, the calibration data for the sensor will already be stored in the application To create the reference image, the position in front of the reference target (see description above) is approached. The correct working distance and centered nozzle position must be ensured.  
· The actual image is created with Image recording
:  
· Then select Configure reference
:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image
text:
· The actual image is created with Image recording
:  
· Then select Configure reference
:
The recording of the reference target shown here represents the maximum possible point distribution, but this is not absolutely necessary in practice. It is important that the reference image and the image sequence from the inspection do not differ, or only differ within the set max. deviation [px]. To allow the deviation to be classified more easily, the specification in [mm] is also displayed, converted in accordance with the permanently stored calibration data for the sensor. If the deviation stated in px is exceeded, the check will result in an error NOK.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image
text:
As soon as the **Configuration** dialog is opened, the quality of the current image recording is checked. For optimal display of the pixels, the brightness threshold can be varied in **manual** mode. The default is that the circle points can already be displayed in automatic mode. The more circle points in the image are displayed in green and the camera positioning is shown vertically below each other, the better the reference image settings.  
In most cases, it is sufficient to optimize the point search by means of the brightness threshold so that the default values for the point filtering can be retained.  
· Click OK to accept the reference configuration and then transmit it to the current distance
value of the sensor.
· To do this, select **Reference sensor**
to open the corresponding dialog and enter the
values **Search radius** and real **Work distance** in [mm].
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image
text:
to open the corresponding dialog and enter the
values **Search radius** and real **Work distance** in [mm].
· Next, define the tolerance value for the permitted **maximum offset**
of the nozzle
position in [px]. This is defined once during configuration and is essential for the result of
the automatic application in the server later on. To allow the deviation to be classified
more easily, the specification in [mm] is displayed underneath, converted in accordance with the permanently stored calibration data for the sensor.
· When you exit the dialog by clicking OK,
the reference image is automatically saved in
a directory created by the system and subsequently used as a reference for every target check called.
· After completion of the configuration in the client, the target application, as with every
other application, can be transmitted to the server.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Step 2: Procedure - Configuring The Reference Image

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
If, for example, deviations with a tolerance of less than 4 px (~1 mm)  are acceptable, the server result is OK for the target check. If the deviation of the calculated nozzle position is greater than 4 px, the server result after completion of the inspection would be NOK. This is generally due to inaccuracy in one of the system components and should be checked before the start of production.  
However, before making corrections to the hardware components, the error message from the target check should be verified with respect to the severity of the error and its relevance.  
The error message NOK is output on the server side with no additional information. Here, the RTVision.tclient offers the option of an analysis or reconfiguration for the target check.  
· If the result is NOK on the server side, load the automatically saved error image
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Result Interpretation - Check Errors!

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
· If the result is NOK on the server side, load the automatically saved error image
sequence by clicking the **Load error image** button.
· By clicking **Load reference image**, the image originally saved with the reference
configuration can be loaded and viewed again.
· By clicking the **Compare sensor** button you can compare the set parameters with the
current reference image and analyze the accurate px result at a glance.
This table shows the position values of the nozzle tip including the calculated deviation in [px] and thus allows the error relevance to be assessed for an NOK result.  
· If the deviation is below the set max. deviation, you can end the inspection with OK. · If the error is acceptable, you can reconfigure the reference by increasing the maximum
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Result Interpretation - Check Errors!

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
deviation [px]. A reference with a new deviation is configured, which then leads to an OK result as a new reference (image).
· However, if the maximum deviation is no longer acceptable, the complete system must
be checked for incorrect adjustment of the individual hardware components.
Avoid manual recalibration of the sensor if possible. A negative target check is not sufficient grounds for manual calibration of the sensor. It only shows deviations with respect to an original reference. The sensor is not calibrated in this case. A clear distinction must be made here.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Result Interpretation - Check Errors!

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The repair function requires extended signal exchange between the inspection system RTVision.t and the systems involved in the process, such as glue controller, robot controller and higher level PLC. By way of illustration, these systems should be collectively treated as a higher-level controller. Like the inspection itself, the repair is performed on the basis of the product. In addition to supplying the product result, the inspection system signals to the higher-level controller whether a part that returned NOK can be repaired. The higher-level controller repeats the component program (robot path) if repair is required. It also signals to the inspection system that the most recently inspected component is to be repaired
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.2 Repair Interface (Licensed Option)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . It also signals to the inspection system that the most recently inspected component is to be repaired. The inspection system then provides information about the points on the bead where the applicator nozzle needs to be opened for the purpose of reworking the bead; this information is provided for the repair run as an output signal.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.2 Repair Interface (Licensed Option)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
|   Bit offset | Signal output     | Signal input   |
|--------------|-------------------|----------------|
|            0 | Product reparable | Repair product |
|            1 | Close nozzle      |                |  
Note The additional repair signals happen in the same time frame as the signals of the basic interface PV2.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Signals

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Note The additional repair signals happen in the same time frame as the signals of the basic interface PV2.  
Product reparable (0) Whether a repair is possible is output synchronously with the output signal "Result available" after the first inspection. The signal is reset when the next product is selected. The previous component can no longer be repaired if no repair is performed with the next product selected. Repair product (0) If, when a product is selected, the signal is set and the previous inspection detected a reparable component, the output signal "Close nozzle" can be used for the repair. In addition, the product ID for the repair inspection must not change compared with the first inspection.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Signals

|--------------------------------------------|----------------------------|
| Repair mode active, no product             |                          0 |
| Repair mode active, outside inspection     |                          1 |
| Repair mode active, request "Close nozzle" |                          1 |
| Repair mode active, request "Open nozzle"  |                          0 |

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Special Specifications - Optional / Repair Interface (Licensed Option) Basic Settings For Fieldbus Communication
text:
The repair function is a licensed additional function that can be optionally activated if a license is available. The following values are used for the repair function in the RTVision_t.ini configuration file: [PROTOCOL-V2] OutputStartRepair=<Byte Index>
Mapping of the output byte for repair on the fieldbus
(>=5) InputStartRepair=<Byte Index>
Mapping of the input byte for repair on the fieldbus (>=5)
Note on repair function A repair (additional purchase option) always requires a fresh run-through of the entire robot motion program.  
Note There must be no difference in the robot path, speed and distance to the applicator head between the teach-in run, first application and repair run.  
You will find further information on this in: Defining the repair area
.  
235
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Specifications - Optional / Repair Interface (Licensed Option) Basic Settings For Fieldbus Communication

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note The repair function is an optional enhancement of RTVision.t and must be enabled separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-level controller must also be extended. The associated **Repair mode** tab is only visible if the repair function is enabled. Note The repair is only possible if the function is active for the application, product, inspection and area. This allows the repair to be easily controlled from the superordinate level. The repair settings for the area properties impose further restrictions in terms of repairability.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.2.1 Defining The Repair Area

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
In special cases, a component that returns a NOK result can be upgraded to OK through subsequent automatic reworking (repair) and repeat inspection. However, this option is only available if the error can be fixed by reapplying glue. For example, gaps in a glue bead can be touched up but position errors and violations of the maximum glue width cannot be repaired. The system remembers the position of the faults and can rework them in a second run.  
You can activate the repair function for the selected area on the *Repair mode* tab. The following settings can also be made:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.2.1 Defining The Repair Area

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Special Specifications - Optional / Repair Interface (Licensed Option)
text:
108
Relative error tolerance The error and warning limit values for maximum glue width specified in Error definition can be increased here for the inspection during the repair run. Minimal length of the glue gap Defines the minimum length for categorizing a glue gap as repairable. If this box is not checked, a glue gap will be categorized as always repairable. Maximal width of the seam Defines the maximum width for categorizing a width error as repairable. If this box is not checked, a width error will be categorized as always repairable.  
Note The **Maximal width of the seam** setting is only valid for glue beads that are too thin.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Specifications - Optional / Repair Interface (Licensed Option)

text:
Note The **Maximal width of the seam** setting is only valid for glue beads that are too thin.  
Time delay start point time Defines an offset value in the ms/mm range that moves the start time of the repair to compensate for any delays in the signal flow. Time delay end point Defines an offset value in the ms/mm range that moves the end time of the repair to compensate for any delays in the signal flow. You will find further information on this in:
233
How does the repair interface work?
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Specifications - Optional / Repair Interface (Licensed Option)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The basic interface comprises 4 standard bytes in each case and can be extended through the addition of further bytes to create a customized interface. Some options are provided as an example here, which have been implemented to varying degrees to date together with customers.  
Note - special specifications This involves the implementation of special specifications, which are to be defined between the operator and the manufacturer BEFORE commissioning. Depending on the request, the interface can only be extended in consultation with development and the customer specification; it usually involves the manufacturer adapting the application, rather than arbitrary adjustments on site by the service department.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.3 Interface Extension

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The standard RTVision.t system is a time-based inspection system, whose basic unit is generally based on [ms]. A path-based approach can be followed here by using the robot coordinates provided and communication via n bytes. Here too: This special specification must be defined and programmed in advance; it cannot be integrated as standard.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Robot Path (Only On Request):

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: After a component (product) has been inspected, the results of the individual inspections can be queried (OK, WARN, NOK). After activating the functionality, communication must be configured via the extended interface using n bytes (usually 5). Once this is done, each inspection can be assigned its own inspection ID in configuration mode. The inspection result query can only be issued when the product result is available, it is recognized as valid and the query is then executed according to the inspection ID. Here too: This special specification must be defined and configured in advance; it cannot be integrated as standard.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Inspection Result Query:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The extended interface offers the possibility to define n bytes, in which transmitted or stored seam numbers are taken over and processed internally by the image processing system. Optional specific settings must be made in the system for this purpose by the authorized service partner. See Take over seam numbers
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Take Over Seam Numbers: 242

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The extended interface offers the possibility to define n bytes, which include transmitted serial numbers in the result data. Optional specific settings must be made in the system for this purpose by the authorized service partner. The data type (ASCII, hexadecimal or integers) must be defined, while the start byte for the serial number and the maximum length of the serial number must likewise be specified.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Transferring Serial Numbers:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The existing basic interface can be extended depending on the type and scope of the customer data to be received, transferred or made available. The objective, data type(s) and data length must be defined beforehand. Different numbers of bytes and differently named bytes can be used for this purpose, so that here, too, a special specification must be defined in advance. Activation or integration via the authorized service partner is not possible without prior consultation and, if necessary, adjustment by the manufacturer.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Customer Data:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
RTVision.t communicates with the glue controller used via the extended interface link.  
Depending on the configuration of the corresponding software program, signals can be exchanged and, if necessary, communicated with the aid of color-coded signaling. This option has to be specified in advance however and cannot be configured subsequently on the installed RTVision.t system. By default, the RTVision.t is the higher-level communication partner in this case. This is visualized on the software interface on the server screen with an appropriately labeled button. This button allows a direct switch from the RTVision.t server to the glue controller software and indicates (depending on the glue controller software used) basic messages by a change of color.  
| Button                               | Meaning                              | Action                              |
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.4 Communicating With The Glue Controller

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
To create a new inspection, click Add.  A new entry with a default name in the defined local language appears in the list of inspections.  
65
Note Unlike products, inspections are not numbered via the robot controller. They are always executed in the order in which they appear in the list of inspections. More information here
.  
This specific requirement allows the seam numbers to be transferred here as inspection names. The **Take over seam numbers** button below the inspection list is active if the associated image sequence has been loaded. Having acknowledged the confirmation prompt, the seam numbers that were added with the image sequences in the server are transferred as inspection names. A seam number is linked with the sequence and transferred from the robot controller for each image sequence.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.5 Take Over Seam Numbers

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Note Note that for parameterization in RTVision.t, the system must already be definitively functional. Robot speed, glue control and travel paths must be installed as defined in the production conditions so that final parameterization of the products can take place in RTVision.t. If the robot speed is changed, for example, following parameterization, the product must be taught in again, reparameterized if necessary and the error map adapted to the new start positions of the areas.  
On the server side, a message below the inspection result (in Simple statistics) indicates the extent to which the seam number assigned to the image sequence matches the seam number originally transferred. The message is only displayed if deviations are determined in the seam number; however, this information is not part of the inspection and does not influence the inspection result.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.5 Take Over Seam Numbers

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The RTVision.t system allows a number of files to be provided for loading on / collection by the customer's MQTT system thanks to its comprehensive interface extension. The program provided separately for this purpose monitors the data generated by RTVision.t at directory level, extracts the required information and sends it to the predefined server. This extended data storage allows the data requested by the customer to be made available for MQTT (using the MQ Telemetry Transport) via a simple dialog. As an external auxiliary tool, this product is reserved for the service department and is delivered with German and English interface only. Additional programming effort is likewise needed for RTVision.t to provide the data, which means that a standard procedure is not used here. Hence, the previous definition of how the data transfer works has to be defined together in advance
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.6 Data Transfer Via Mqtt

Tip - Clarify the desired configuration together in advance Prior to commissioning therefore, discuss your desired configuration with the unlicensed specifications.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Ghost mode is an important tool for local commissioning staff because important system functions, particularly for communication and hardware, can be tested without products needing to be parameterized fully. Results are output that show the correct system functionality, though they are not recorded in the statistics data. This means that the image processing system has no impact on the production process during ghost mode. The
247
applicable icon in this case is shown in large form in automatic mode (server) when the mode is active. See Ghost mode
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
252
The data can be transferred optionally from the directory system to the PostgreSQL database, which is supplied on request. Its use is not necessary for single systems. This is also the case with networked systems, but if there is a requirement for database storage then this option is possible from V.4.8. See Optional database usage
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Optional Database Usage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
RTVision.t can verify and accept access authorization via electronic key systems (e.g.  
Euchner key). No entry is needed in the user management in this case. However, if access to the software is required independently of an Electronic Key System (EKS) for security reasons (for example, in the case of a deputizing arrangement), the employee should be managed in the user management with their access authorization and password assignment as a precaution, as otherwise there is no possibility to access RTVision.t without the Electronic Key System.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Electronic Access Control

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: On the right of the toolbar at the top, an additional button can be displayed in the server module if required, which can be used to open Windows Explorer with password protection. This is a good way to open Explorer specifically without a keyboard at touch-operated workstations.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Explorer Button (Server)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Ui Interface - Choosing Between Two Different Layouts
text:
The RTVision.t software has been redesigned with regard to its appearance and now offers a modern work area in two different color layouts for clear handling.  
The switchover is carried out in the system after commissioning by the authorized service partner.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Ui Interface - Choosing Between Two Different Layouts

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: By activating the relevant variable, the language setting for the installed RTVision.t server, client and viewer modules can also be made here globally. Once activated, each module to be opened adopts the language setting of the first module opened, which means that there is no need to change the language individually any more. Please contact the authorized service partner in this regard.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Central Language Setting

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Default Setting For Full Screen And Window Mode
text: Depending on requirements, full screen or scalable window mode can be set by means of a variable for working in production. If window mode is preferred, the module opens and always positions itself at the same positions and in the sizes as when previously closed. Please contact the authorized service partner in this regard.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Default Setting For Full Screen And Window Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Ghost mode plays an important role in the course of on-site commissioning. The system functions are tested with regard to  
· Communication between the glue controller and RTVision.t, · Hardware signals, · Parameter test for target check (sensor comparison).
Ghost mode runs without being linked to the other production processes and can be activated without a product already having been created and taught in. The result can be ERROR or OK. If the status of communication and hardware is OK, an OK is always displayed as a result in the RTVision.t server.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.7.1 Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: This OK does not refer to a product / component. Existing statistics data is therefore not influenced. All component testing is bypassed in Ghost mode and a result of OK is output, which is not recorded via the statistics. The extent to which the three tested components are faulty, missing or functioning perfectly in terms of their signaling is indicated by a red or green status signal in the Commissioning dialog. The status is displayed here. In addition, it is possible to output these statuses with a date and time stamp using a pdf short report. These reports are saved under **Report | Service**. With the result OK, all technologies including the RTVision.tsystem are ready to run and active at the end of commissioning - a proven means, for example, to bypass the image processing inspection system temporarily in the course of performance tests, without switching it off.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.7.1 Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note - Installation of hardware complete - Verification in ghost mode Assuming that the RTVision.t system is fully installed and connected with its hardware, both the RTVision.t server and client modules should be started. At this point, no product (not even sensor comparison/target check) need have been created as yet. Lack of parameterization does not lead to an error in ghost mode on the server.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.7.1 Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
1. Click **Connect** to establish a connection from the client to the server. This is a
prerequisite for making further settings on the server.
2. Two of the three inspection options, i.e. **Hardware** and **Communication**, are also
displayed with regard to the current status in the status bar on the right-hand side of the server.
If a problem is already apparent here, it will also exist in the later Commissioning dialog. That's because this dialog also only shows the states of the hardware and the communication with color coding.  
3. Go to the main menu in the RTVision.t server and select **Service | Commissioning**. If
the connections are still incomplete, this is immediately displayed in the program status area:
4. If these factors are green, ghost mode can usually result in an OK in the server without
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Procedure For Activating Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
4. If these factors are green, ghost mode can usually result in an OK in the server without
any problems. The mode can also be activated for red factors, but an error is output after activation in the event of faulty communication or hardware.
Fig.:  Two constellations lead to different results on the server  
5. Click **Activate** to activate ghost mode on the server with password protection and
administrator rights only.
The ghost symbols are displayed in the server view when automatic mode is active in a clearly recognizable way. If all components are still okay, the result is OK. However, no result from ghost mode is included in the overall statistics.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Procedure For Activating Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note - Ghost mode also outside commissioning Ghost mode is not only useful during commissioning. Since no results from component tests are changed, ghost mode can also be activated during various performance tests, for example. The image processing system does not have to be deactivated, actual results are not falsified and at the same time the functioning of signal communication is checked with respect to the abovementioned factors.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Procedure For Activating Ghost Mode

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Special Specifications - Optional / Additional Unlicensed Specifications Interpretation Of Results
text:
An error is output if there is a hardware error within the image processing system or if there are problems with communication between the inspection system and the glue controller.  
The corresponding message is also recorded in the logbook.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Specifications - Optional / Additional Unlicensed Specifications Interpretation Of Results

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The sensor comparison is a specific product created on the client that performs a target check using a special referencing plate by means of native parameterization. Ghost mode now checks whether this functionality is already set up, but also whether it may have led to a NOK. The status Ready or Not ready is checked here. Both lead to an OK in the ghost process, since the red traffic light signal should not lead to an interruption or stop in the system during the sensor comparison. It is purely a question here of querying the status of the "Target" product.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Special Case Of Sensor Comparison

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Click **Create report** to finally create a commissioning report, which is stored in the Report / Service folder as a pdf file called Commissioning_Date_Time.pdf. This report shows the status of the three factors mentioned and includes a date and time stamp. Deactivate ghost mode to revert to the regular test sequence for the components.  
You will find further information on this in: Special specification of target check / sensor comparison 226
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Commissioning Report

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
RTVision.t generally uses data storage solely as a file system structure. Now for the first time, Version 4.8 also offers a free option for transferring and storing data in a database via the file system.  
The database storage feature is useful in the case of network integration of multiple systems. The database can be filled with all actual and target data from the image processing software, but image sequences cannot be stored there. In the case of single systems, the database does not offer any real advantage, rather you can work purely via the directory storage as usual.  
Note - Database connection The configuration may only be carried out by the authorized service partner, as the system must be configured at several points with in-depth settings. Before commissioning, clarify the data storage you intend to use with the commissioning engineer.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.7.2 Optional Database Usage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Tools are provided by the manufacturer for the configuration: RTVision.t Server RTVision.t Client RTVision.t Viewer RTVision.t Publish (additional program) PostgreSQL DB (Third party product) The **Publish** program provided separately for this purpose monitors the data generated by RTVision.t at directory level, extracts the required information and sends it to the predefined server. The database is installed there and the link between server and database is also set up via the additional **Publish** program. This additional program therefore configures the directory monitoring, the transfer and also the clean-up of the data in the database. If the **Login to the server** button is grayed out, the connection between database and server is established
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.7.2 Optional Database Usage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . If the **Login to the server** button is grayed out, the connection between database and server is established. By clicking **Close**, the additional program is not exited, rather the configuration dialog is simply closed and the program continues to run in the background. If the additional program is to be exited, the list of hidden icons should be opened via the task bar. Right-clicking the program icon opens the menu for exiting RTVision.t Publish.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8.7.2 Optional Database Usage

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Chapter 9 Appendix 9 Appendix 9.1 Care And Maintenance
text: Note - Material damage The RTVision.t  system must be cleaned and inspected regularly to ensure proper operation. Servicing and maintenance work where the sensor head is accessed is possible only during a break in production. Do not change the position of the sensor to avoid having to readjust the application parameters. While cleaning, do not damage the special transparent screen of the sensor (e.g. fine scratches).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Chapter 9 Appendix 9 Appendix 9.1 Care And Maintenance

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Due to air exchange, over time the filter mats of the PC fan can become very dirty and must be cleaned at regular intervals. To do this, open the lock on the front of the computer and then open the two covers. Inside the covers you will see a fan filter which you should clean with a vacuum cleaner. the manufacturer is that you check for dirt every week. The level of dirt, however, depends very much on the surrounding conditions. We would therefore ask that the intervals be adjusted according to prior experience with maintenance.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Cleaning The Fan Filters (Computer)

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Cleaning The Sensor Note Normally There Is No Need To Clean The Lens In Rtvision.T Because Of Its Design.
text:
The lenses have a special surface coat to prevent mirroring and reflections. A dirty surface on the lenses or other parts in the optical path caused by dust, oil or grease creates shadowing effects. The uniform imaging of the objects is consequently impaired. The lenses are protected behind a special acrylic glass screen, which means that regular cleaning of this screen is generally sufficient. Effects of dirt on the sensor surface are:
· Over time, the recorded images become continuously darker  
· Dark points in some areas of the image · Over time, the recorded images become blurred
the manufacturer is that you check for dirt every week or always if one of the described phenomena occurs.  
Note - Material damage Make sure that the transparent special screen of the sensor is not damaged. Scratches can cause changes in the optical properties or even a complete breakdown.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Cleaning The Sensor Note Normally There Is No Need To Clean The Lens In Rtvision.T Because Of Its Design.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Cleaning The Sensor Note Normally There Is No Need To Clean The Lens In Rtvision.T Because Of Its Design.
text: Do not rub with a dry cloth or clean with chemical solvents. Avoid all cleaning products that could damage the acrylic glass surface. When wiping, avoid applying too much pressure to the surface.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Cleaning The Sensor Note Normally There Is No Need To Clean The Lens In Rtvision.T Because Of Its Design.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
· Use a soft, oil-free paintbrush to remove dust from the sensor surface. · To remove oil, grease or fingerprints from the sensor surface, wipe the dirty area with a
moist clean soft cloth and some household cleaning product and dry the surface. Use a
microfiber cloth or an oil-free, clean soft cotton cloth.
· Repeat the wiping process until the surface of the sensor's cover screen is completely
clean.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Cleaning Process:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!
text:
· When the strobe controller has been switched off, disconnect the cable on the sensor head.  
The sensor optics are protected from environmental influences by an acrylic glass cover screen. Depending on the sensor version, it can also perform the function of a polarizing filter and/or a diffuser. When changing ensure the functionality of the cover screen is identical to the one being replaced. Check the scope of delivery for the replacement screen immediately after delivery. Components include screen with seal and O-ring. As this cover screen is dirtied and therefore also scratched by the production environment, it should be checked regularly and changed as required.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!
text:
Note - Material damage! To remove and replace the cover screen you will require a pair of antistatic gloves (soft material) and an O-ring hook tool to remove the O-ring undamaged from the groove. Make sure that the transparent special screen of the sensor is not damaged. Scratches can cause changes in the optical properties or even a complete breakdown. Work with gloves to minimize the risk of damaging the new cover screen.  
1. Maintenance work with access to the sensor head is possible only during a break in production.  
28
2. When the application has been shut down,
switch off the LED light controller (strobe
controller). 3. Then disconnect the cable on the sensor head. 4. You do not have to disconnect the sensor from the robot arm to change the cover screen.
260
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!
text:
260
However, should you be forced to do so because of poor accessibility, please always observe the information concerning the sensor position in section Replacing the sensor hardware
.  
5. Grip the O-ring with the O-ring hook tool and pull it carefully out of the sensor's housing groove. Now remove the seal ring and then remove the cover screen. Have the new O-ring and the new cover screen ready.  
6. Place the new cover screen on the open sensor surface.
Ensure you do not damage any of the LEDs, cameras or the PCB on the inside here. Also handle cover screens with polarization film or diffuser coating especially carefully. Make sure that the lug on the cover screen and the housing recess engage with each other.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 9.1.1 Replacing The Sensor Cover Danger Of Electric Shock!
text:
When positioning, the thin sponge rubber seal on the edge of the cover screen must face inwards to efficiently protect the optical components in the production environment against dirt. The cover screen is inserted with the seal side facing downward so that the seal rests on the inner housing level. The cover screen is properly positioned when it is completely inserted into the sensor housing.  
7. Thread the O-ring over the axis and ensure that the O-ring in the housing groove grips evenly across its entire area, so that the new cover screen sits correctly.  
8. Then clean the cover screen with a clean, soft and lint-free cotton cloth. 9. To start the system up again, connect the cable with the sensor head, then start the strobe controller and wait approx. 20 s until the camera system starts again. 10. Then start the RTVision.t server and client software.

section: 9.1.2 Replacing The Sensor Hardware Danger Of Electric Shock!
text:
Make sure that the transparent special screen of the sensor is not damaged. Scratches can cause changes in the optical properties or even a complete breakdown. All sensors are designed for a certain working distance. The replaced physical sensor must have the same configuration so that no further settings are required. However, a renewed teach-in may be necessary in some circumstances. If you replace the existing sensor with a different type of sensor, a complete new parameterization is required. If you replace the existing sensor with the same sensor type, it is recommended that you perform sensor referencing with the referencing plate (software option, target check). Clarify the issue with the manufacturer beforehand and, if necessary, contact the authorized service provider.  
1. Maintenance work with access to the sensor head is possible only during a break in production.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Note - Identical Position Of Replacement Sensor
text:
Make sure that the new sensor is positioned exactly like the old one. This is necessary to ensure that the camera position, working distance and search radius are identical.  
12 position markings are embossed in the sensor housing (see graphic sensor numbering). On the flange of the sensor there is a corresponding groove (blue arrow). The three screws are marked with the red arrows. This ensures easily identifiable positioning.  
5. During assembly, observe the correct positioning and the maximum permissible tightening torque of 0.7 Nm for the Allen screws.  
6. Secure the screws with locking fluid. 7. Connect the new sensor with the electric cabling. 8. Switch on the LED light controller (strobe controller).
Wait about 20 seconds until the camera system starts.  
9. Then start the RTVision.t server and client software.
262

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: 6. If A Sensor Is Defective And Has To Be Replaced, Click **Replace Sensor**.
text: 7. Since the application is changed in the system, a message is output that the sensor calibration data stored in the application will be replaced by the data of the new sensor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 6. If A Sensor Is Defective And Has To Be Replaced, Click **Replace Sensor**.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The calibration data of the new sensor is loaded into the application and the new serial number of the sensor camera (internal) is entered. The data in the server and in the client is now identical. At the same time, **Identical calibration** changes to green to show that the calibration data has been applied. 9. Before you confirm this for this application file, click the **Display calibration** button in the Properties *dialog* to compare the *global properties* (opening angle, search radius, working distance) If the sensor type is the same, **Working distance** and **Search radius** should be the same.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8. Confirm By Clicking Yes.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note - Manual calibration! - an extremely rare option If the global properties differ from the replaced model after linking the sensor, the system has to be taught in again. If the deviations for the saved sensor-specific calibration are too big, manual calibration is the last option for overwriting the manufacturer-defined calibration on site. Even then, it is only possible if the sensor link is deleted and it is intended to perform manual calibration via image recording. Never perform manual calibration without prior consultation with and approval by the authorized service provider. The manufacturer accepts no liability for any damage resulting from independently performed manual calibration of the sensor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8. Confirm By Clicking Yes.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: If necessary, you can cancel the process or save the application under a different name and then transmit it. 10. If the data broadly matches, click OK. 11. Save the updated application and transmit it to the RTVision.t server. A new image sequence is created with the next component run, verified automatically at product start-up for checking the hardware status and verified according to the transmitted application. The result of this process shows the extent to which new parameterization is required.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8. Confirm By Clicking Yes.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: 12. Because interaction of the individual components (robot, sensor, adhesive control unit, nozzle) changes slightly when the sensor is replaced, a target check can be performed - if this option was acquired and is active. For this purpose, the referencing plate is approached based on an existing inspection program with specific ID, an image is created and this image is compared with the available reference image. If there are deviations within the system because of one of the components, the server will output NOK after completion of the test program. Before continuing with production, the individual components (robot, gripper arm, adhesive control unit, nozzle) of the referenced complete system should be checked. Even slight tilting of the sensor, for example, will cause a deviation between the new image and the reference image from the old configuration
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8. Confirm By Clicking Yes.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . This can be rectified and the test process repeated. However, there may also be damage to the nozzle or to the other system components causing this NOK result. With **Load error image**, you can analyze the error and thus assess the error relevance. Check this before adjusting the hardware components.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 8. Confirm By Clicking Yes.

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
section: Note - Sensor Calibration Is Not The Same As A Target Check Appendix / Care And Maintenance
text:
Note that manual sensor calibration and a target check are completely different processes, but are implemented in dialogs of the exact same name. Control is performed via different areas and buttons in the **Adjust sensor** dialog.  
A negative target check is not sufficient grounds for manual calibration of the sensor. It only shows deviations with respect to an original reference, which may be hardware-related, but may not necessarily be due to incorrect calibration. Calibration of the sensor is preset at the factory and will not be changed.  
You will find further information on this in:
267
226
Hardware state Target check / sensor comparison as a special specification
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Note - Sensor Calibration Is Not The Same As A Target Check Appendix / Care And Maintenance

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
The basic interface of RTVision.t (V.4.6) now also offers signal output for "Hardware ready". The functionality of the hardware (strobe controller, sensor camera and input / output cable connection) is thus tested automatically when the system is restarted but also before components are inspected. If a NOK is output by this test, this is also visible in automatic mode of the server but no explanation is output for the fault.  
The *Hardware state* dialog shown by opening the main menu under Service | Hardware state can provide assistance here.  
Note Format Note The <%PRODUCT_NAME_3D%> sensor is part of the licensing key. Furthermore, the software can only work in automatic mode when connected to the sensor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > 9.1.4 Hardware State

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: Note that the hardware check is only performed automatically at product startup, before the image is recorded. Manual queries should not be issued while component tests are ongoing since they disrupt execution of the test.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 >

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: The functionality of the connection between the sensor and strobe controller is checked manually using the **Update hardware status** button in the **Hardware state** dialog. Note here that no data stored on the hardware is read or assessed in this context, rather the "Hardware ready" query is processed without serial numbers or calibration data. Pressing the **Reconnect the hardware** button, on the other hand, closes the connection completely, reads the data including the serial numbers and calibration data stored on the hardware and re-establishes the connection with the system. Example: The cable connection to the sensor is interrupted. The query in the dialog with **Update hardware status** aborts after a few seconds with the message **The sensor camera is not accessible**. The serial number is displayed unchanged, however, since no query is issued in this case
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Manual Queries:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text: . The serial number is displayed unchanged, however, since no query is issued in this case. If the **Reconnect the hardware** button is selected, on the other hand, the message The sensor camera is not accessible is issued and a serial number is not detected. The system tried here to read the data from the hardware and since there was no cable connection, the message issued is as described.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Manual Queries:

chapter: Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8
text:
Tip If the strobe controller or sensor has been replaced, it can be checked here whether the hardware's own data was read and stored when the system was restarted. In the event that the sensor is replaced, make sure also that the correct calibration data for the new sensor was also recorded in the client. The sensor link should be updated in this case with the **Replace sensor** button.  
You will find further information on this in:
262
179
Sensor replacement software setting Menu commands in automatic mode
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > Inline Glue And Sealant Application Inspection System Rtvision.T - V. 4.8 > Manual Queries:
